title: 2026 - Expert – Multimodal Content Intelligence
description: **Location: Dublin, Ireland** **About Huawei** Huawei is a leading global provider of information and communications technology (ICT) infrastructure and smart devices. We are committed to bringing digital to every person, home and organization for a fully connected, intelligent world. At Huawei, we have two key drivers of innovation: science and technology, and customer needs. Both commercial value and market demands are driving our innovation and determining how we invest in science and technology. Breakthroughs in technology, in return, stimulate customer needs and allow us to create greater value for customers. **About The IRC** Huawei Ireland Research Centre's (IRC) mission is to position Huawei as a recognised technology leader and global information and communications technology (ICT) solutions provider. We are building an industry\-recognised multi\-disciplinary Research Centre of experts focusing on medium\-term to long\-term issues to achieve this. The IRC will work closely with an open, innovative ecosystem with Huawei customers to address real\-world issues. The IRC will also engage with key European universities to build a basic research capability to support Huawei technical projects. **About The Job** The Terminal Cloud Technology Lab is seeking for a visionary Technical Leader to pioneer the next generation of content intelligence. In this role, you will build and lead a high\-performing local algorithm team dedicated to solving complex multimodal understanding challenges across video, audio, image, and text. Leveraging cutting\-edge Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs), you will architect a self\-evolving content analysis ecosystem. Your work will directly empower our core recommendation engine to achieve a transformative leap in product performance and user engagement. **Responsibilities** * Define and Lead Frameworks: Establish multimodal content intelligence frameworks focused on semantic understanding, narrative reasoning, and high\-level content interpretation across video, text, image, and audio. * Roadmap Ownership: Define the mid\-to\-long\-term technical strategy for content understanding, ensuring alignment between state\-of\-the\-art AI research and core business growth objectives. * Architect Scalable Systems: Build and oversee large\-scale systems for automated semantic tagging, categorization, and content classification to replace traditional manual processes. * Global Collaboration: Define the long\-term technical direction and work closely with global research, product, and engineering teams to integrate content intelligence into core recommendation and discovery systems. * Team Leadership: Build and mentor a world\-class local team of AI/ML experts, fostering a culture of technical excellence and product\-driven innovation. * Help the team solve high\-impact technical challenges such as: \- build a self\-evolving tagging system that supports open\-vocabulary recognition and automated reconstruction of existing tag trees to match rapid business growth. \-model popularity and trend prediction by identifying highlights and dramatic elements in videos, or rhythmic and melodic patterns in music, to forecast distribution performance. * architect a comprehensive quality evaluation system to filter and identify premium content across diverse categories, such as mini\-dramas, spatial audio, and images. **Basic Qualifications** * PhD in Computer Science, AI, Machine Learning or related field * 10\+ years experience in AI / ML research or advanced engineering * Strong experience in several of the following areas: \- Multimodal learning (video, text, image, audio) \- Video understanding, temporal modelling, or sequence modelling \- NLP / text understanding (e.g. content quality, news stream analysis, semantic evaluation) \- Content classification, tagging, recommendation, or search systems * Hands\-on experience with deep learning frameworks such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, or JAX * Experience working with multimodal or vision\-language models (VLM / MLLM) * Experience building or deploying large\-scale AI systems in production environments * Strong understanding of evaluation methodologies, data pipelines, or content intelligence systems **Preferred Qualifications** * Research Excellence: A strong publication record in top\-tier international conferences (e.g., CVPR, ICCV, NeurIPS). * Proven Impact: Experience building large\-scale content understanding platforms or automated tagging systems from scratch. * Passionate about staying at the forefront of Generative AI and Multimodal LLMs. Check out Life at Huawei Ireland Research Centre: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v\=3gR64sYSnOA\&feature\=youtu.be DUE TO THE HIGH VOLUME OF REPLIES, ONLY CANDIDATES WHO ARE SHORTLISTED FOR INTERVIEW WILL BE CONTACTED. **Privacy Statement** Please read and understand our West European Recruitment Privacy Notice before submitting your personal data to Huawei so that you fully understand how we process and manage your personal data received. http://career.huawei.com/reccampportal/portal/hrd/weu\_rec\_all.html

title: 2026 Ireland Graduate Civil Engineering Career Path: Water Sector
description: **We will close for applications when we have filled the role and therefore we recommend applying as soon as possible.** Before making an application, please browse all graduate opportunities available as we will only accept **one application** per candidate. Once you have made an application you will not be able to make changes to your selection or make a new application. The exception is if we open new vacancies in the new year. **To avoid missing out on your preferred role, we recommend applying as early as possible, as positions will close once filled.** Turn your ambition into action \- apply now and shape your future with us. **Job Description** Designing Resilient Infrastructure for a Sustainable Future. Join a team that’s shaping tomorrow’s water systems—today. At Mott MacDonald, our Water Division is a centre of excellence, tackling some of the most pressing challenges of our time: climate change, ageing infrastructure, and population growth. As a Graduate Civil Engineer, you’ll play a vital role in designing safe, sustainable water and wastewater systems that serve communities now and for generations to come. This is more than a graduate role—it’s an opportunity to grow, collaborate, and make a meaningful impact. You’ll be supported by a caring and inclusive team, empowered to innovate, and inspired to contribute to projects that improve lives and protect the environment. What You’ll Be Doing You’ll work alongside multidisciplinary teams of engineers, scientists, and consultants, contributing to projects that blend technical excellence with social purpose. Your responsibilities may include: * Designing Infrastructure \- Contribute to the design of pipelines, water and wastewater treatment facilities across feasibility, outline, and detailed design stages. * Collaborative Delivery \- Work with diverse teams to develop integrated, sustainable solutions that reflect the needs of communities and ecosystems. * Project Lifecycle Support \- Be involved from planning and modelling through to construction, commissioning, and handover—gaining holistic project experience. * Digital Innovation \- Use cutting\-edge digital tools to enhance design quality, efficiency, and collaboration. Why This Role Matters * Empowering Growth \- You’ll receive mentoring, structured training, and hands\-on experience to build your technical and project management skills. * Celebrating Impact \- Every project you contribute to helps deliver clean water, protect natural resources, and build resilience against climate change. * Inclusive Culture \- We celebrate diversity and foster a culture where everyone feels valued, heard, and supported. * Integrity \& Trust \- We uphold the highest standards of professionalism and ethics, building trust with clients, communities, and each other. * Wellbeing First \- Your wellbeing is our priority. We offer flexible working, mental health support, and a caring environment where you can thrive. * Purpose\-Driven Work \- Your engineering solutions will have real\-world impact—enhancing public health, environmental sustainability, and community resilience. **Candidate Specification** We are looking for committed and motivated graduates with a genuine passion and a desire to make a difference in the world. If this describes you, apply today to launch your career at Mott MacDonald. To be eligible for this opportunity, you will have less than 12 months’ relevant experience **(excluding placements)** and have a bachelor's degree (obtained no earlier than 2024\) or expected to achieve a degree in **civil engineering** or similar disciplines: In your application, you should aim to demonstrate your genuine interest in both the opportunity and in joining Mott MacDonald. You can do this by highlighting any relevant experience or interests \- such as modules you've studied, previous work experience, or any research you've undertaken about our company, values, and projects. We personally review every completed application form we receive as this part of our selection process is entirely human led – we do not use AI tools at this stage and kindly expect you not to either. We want you to answer each application question with authenticity so we can get to know the real you. Joining us as a graduate is your opportunity to shape your own story. We are looking for graduates with the following strengths: * Proactively takes initiative to complete tasks efficiently and independently. * Communicates effectively and clearly with clients and colleagues. * Delivers high quality written work with strong attention to detail and clarity. * Demonstrates a structured and analytical approach to problem solving. **About Your Development** A graduate position should be more than just a job. We know this and so do you. That’s why with our graduate roles we aim to give you the experience and technical knowledge you need to progress your career. From the moment you join us, you’ll receive the training you need. You will be assigned a mentor, who will guide you to meet the objectives of your professional training needs. You will join our three\-year early careers soft skills development programme – Accelerating your Future, which is designed to give you the skills and tools necessary for a long\-lasting successful career here at Mott MacDonald. You will have the opportunity to make a difference; learn more about Our Purpose and the difference we can make! You’re probably wondering what else is on offer. Join us, and you’ll get: * Biannual salary reviews: we believe that hard work should be rewarded and recognised. Therefore, for the first three years of your career with us, you’ll have biannual salary reviews. * A competitive salary: in addition to biannual reviews, we will ensure that you’re given a salary that matches the current industry standard. **Our social side** Being part of Mott MacDonald means more than just work; there’s a huge range of fun and exciting things that you can get involved in. From the moment you join us, you’ll have the opportunity to expand your social and professional network, whether it’s meeting other graduates or joining forces with other members of staff from around the company at our annual sports day. Each of our offices have a sports and social committee which will give you the chance to be part of a variety of sports, social and charity events. We’re committed to promoting a strong culture of social responsibility and encourage our staff to play active roles in the local community. It's worth noting that sports and social committees tend to vary from office to office, so as well as getting involved in what’s already on offer, don’t be afraid to suggest new events or initiatives that you think could be a great addition. It doesn’t stop there. As well as events, you’ll also have access to discounts on cinema tickets, travel, fashion, utilities and much more as part of our company benefits which you can take advantage of the moment you join. **Equality, diversity and inclusion** We put equality, diversity and inclusion at the heart of our business, seeking to promote fair employment procedures and practices to ensure equal opportunities for all. We encourage individual expression in our workplace and are committed to creating an inclusive environment where everyone feels they have the opportunity to contribute. **Agile working** At Mott MacDonald, we believe it makes business sense for you and your manager to choose how you can work most effectively to meet your client, team and personal commitments. We embrace agility, flexibility and trust. **More About Mott MacDonald** We’re a global engineering, management and development consultancy. Our purpose is to improve society by considering social outcomes in everything we do, relentlessly focusing on excellence and digital innovation, transforming our clients’ businesses, our communities and employee opportunities. A fundamental part of this is respecting each person’s differences and striving to meet their needs. Our values: progress, respect, integrity, drive, excellence Location(s): Dublin, IE Contract Type: Permanent Work Pattern: Full Time Market: Water Discipline: Civil Job Ref: 11504 Recruiter Contact: earlycareers.recruitment@mottmac.com

title: A Fresh Start for Your Career in Recruitment Consulting
description: * **Channel your drive into a role that fuels a company in rapid expansion.** * **Your effort sets the pace – fast and completely performance\-based career progression.** * **Join a business that rewards ambition and invests in your future.** What if your career moved forward exactly as fast as you’re willing to push it? What if you didn’t have to wait for someone else’s approval to take the next step? At **Access** , your results speak louder than anything else. Here, **progression isn’t about time served, it’s about performance** . If you bring energy, resilience, and hunger to succeed, there’s no ceiling on what you can achieve. Maybe you’re already in recruitment and ready for a step up. Or maybe you’re looking for a new challenge: a role where ambition, people skills, and results are truly rewarded. Either way, this is your chance to build something exciting. **What the role involves:** As a Recruitment Consultant, you’ll be backed by a leadership team dedicated to helping you grow quickly and confidently. Every day brings variety and purpose: * Building relationships with clients and candidates across the healthcare sector * Managing live vacancies from first briefing to final offer * Interviewing, qualifying, and advising candidates through each stage of the process **What’s in it for you:** * **Career Progression** \- Rise fast through a clear, merit\-based pathway into senior and leadership positions. * **Continuous Development** \- Access expert mentorship, hands\-on training, and specialist knowledge to accelerate your career. * **Recognition \& Rewards** \- Every milestone matters. Expect real recognition, added responsibility, and outstanding financial rewards. The **average total compensation for year 1 is more than €45k, and for year 2 it’s over €66k** . Of course, due to our uncapped commission structure, top performers earn much more than this from the jump. * **Leadership Academy** \- Exclusive access to our Leadership Academy for advanced training, certifications, and networking with top performers. **You’ll be a great fit if you have:** * At least 1 year of experience in a fast\-moving environment (any sector) * A proactive, ambitious mindset and the confidence to take ownership of your growth * Strong communication skills and a natural drive to succeed **Your success story could look like Rian’s:** In just **24 months** , he progressed from Recruitment Consultant **to Associate Manager** , building a high\-performing team and shaping his own leadership path. In two years, you could be his peer, and you could be telling this same story to your friends, but this time about yourself. For more information, contact César Toscano at **\+353 1 572 1161** or email your CV to **cesar.toscano@accesshealthcare.ie**

title: AI/ML Agent Engineer — Front-End Focus
description: At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts. Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet. Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers — and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all. ****Role Overview**** We’re hiring a Junior AI/ML Agent Engineer with a front\-end focus to build agentic workflows and tools that integrate LLMs, OpenAI specs, MCP, and modern frontend frameworks. You’ll collaborate with product and platform teams to deliver intuitive web experiences that harness AI agents for developer and business workflows. ****Key Responsibilities**** * Design and implement agentic workflows demonstrating LLMs (Claude, OpenAI), MCP clients/servers, and OpenAI specifications for tool/function orchestration. * Build user\-facing features in AngularJS and ReactJS, with services and tooling in Node.js and Python for agent integration. * Implement prompt engineering, tool schemas, and function calling patterns; handle context windows, memory, and retrieval strategies for agent reliability. * Integrate agents with developer ecosystems: GitHub (actions, PR comment bots), Cline (AI coding assistant workflows), and Claude model endpoints. * Develop reusable UI components and SDKs for agent invocation, status visualization, and error handling; contribute to design systems and accessibility. * Ship high\-quality code with unit/e2e tests, CI/CD, and observability (logs, metrics, traces) for agent runs and UI flows. * Document architecture, OpenAI/MCP spec contracts, and developer guides for internal consumers. **Minimum Qualifications** * 2–5 years of professional experience in software engineering with front\-end emphasis. * Hands\-on experience building with LLMs (OpenAI, Claude) and agent/workflow patterns (tools, function calling, retries, guardrails). * Working knowledge of MCP (Model Context Protocol) concepts and implementation of clients/servers. * Proficient in AngularJS and/or ReactJS, plus Node.js and Python for agent backends and tooling. * Familiarity with OpenAI specifications (tool definitions, schemas) and API integration best practices. * Experience integrating with GitHub (Actions, APIs), and exposure to Cline and Claude models and strong fundamentals: TypeScript/JavaScript, REST/GraphQL, testing (Jest/Cypress), linting, and accessibility. **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience with RAG pipelines, embeddings, or lightweight vector stores for context augmentation. * Basic security awareness for agent design: input validation, secrets management, rate limiting, and least\-privilege tokens. * Observability with Datadog/OpenTelemetry or similar for agent runs and UI interactions. * Knowledge of prompt versioning, evaluation (e.g., golden sets), and experiment design. ****What We Value**** * Eagerness to learn and adapt to emerging models/agent frameworks. * Bias for action, pragmatic problem\-solving, and ownership of outcomes. * Clear communication and collaborative mindset across product, design, and infra. ****Salary \& Benefits**** Competitive mid\-level compensation with benefits. Final level and pay depend on experience and interview performance. How to Apply Submit your resume, GitHub/portfolio links, and a brief note describing an agent or AI\-enabled feature you’ve built, including frameworks, models, and evaluation approach **.** **Additional Details** eBay is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, and disability, or other legally protected status. If you have a need that requires accommodation, please contact us at talent@ebay.com. We will make every effort to respond to your request for accommodation as soon as possible. View our accessibility statement to learn more about eBay's commitment to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities. We use cookies to enhance your experience and may use AI tools for administrative tasks in the hiring process. To learn how we handle your personal data and use AI responsibly, please visit our Talent Privacy Notice, Privacy Center, and AI Hiring Guidelines.

title: Architect

title: Associate
description: **🌍Job Title: Associate/Principal Engineer – Water** **Division: Water Engineering** **Location: Dublin/Cork, Ireland** **💡About Us** Ayesa Engineering is a global engineering consultancy with over 13,000 employees, where our UK \& Ireland business combines international strength with the agility and autonomy of an SME. With a presence in 40 countries, Ayesa Engineering has built a strong international reputation across a diverse range of engineering disciplines. Our expertise includes water and flood infrastructure, transportation, marine and coastal engineering, geoscience, environmental services, energy, and building structures. Our UK \& Ireland teams deliver technically challenging, varied projects that develop broad expertise without confining people to narrow roles — offering the opportunity to make a meaningful local impact while benefiting from the stability, knowledge, and career opportunities of a well\-established global organisation. **What You Will Be Doing:** As an Associate/Principal Engineer, you’ll lead and deliver Wastewater and Water design packages across diverse sectors including commercial, residential, education, healthcare, and public infrastructure. We require a self\-driving engineer at senior level to manage predominantly UÉ related work but also to support on our civil designs for contractors. **Key Responsibilities** * As the technical lead, you will ensure all designs meet the specific Standard Details and Codes of Practice set by Uisce Éireann or other relevant authority. * Design Lead: Spearhead the design of water distribution networks, foul sewerage collection systems, pumping stations and general civil engineering. * Hydraulic Modelling: Oversee or perform complex hydraulic analysis for water and wastewater networks using software packages. * Flood Risk: Prepare and review Site\-Specific Flood Risk Assessments in accordance with "The Planning System and Flood Risk Management" guidelines. * Understanding the Irish planning landscape is critical for moving a project from concept to construction. Understanding of the Irish property acquisition and wayleave process. * Planning Submissions: Prepare technical reports and drawings for Part 8 (Local Authority) or Strategic Infrastructure Development (SID) planning applications. * Statutory Liaising: Negotiate with Local Authorities, the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), and Uisce Éireann for connections and/or design diversions. * Environmental Coordination: Collaborate with ecologists / hydrologists / archaeologists to ensure designs comply with Appropriate Assessments (AA) and Environmental Impact Assessment Reports (EIAR). * Contract Documentation: Draft tender documents using the Capital Works Management Framework (CWMF) for public projects or FIDIC/NEC4 for private/utility contracts. * Contract Administration: Act as the "Employer’s Representative" managing claims, variations, and payment certificates. * Procurement: Evaluate tenders and provide technical recommendations to clients for contractor selection. * You will be responsible to work in multidisciplinary teams while ensuring legal safety compliance. * PSDP Role: Fulfil the duties of the Project Supervisor for the Design Process (PSDP) under the 2006 Safety, Health and Welfare at Work (Construction) Regulations. * Financial Oversight: Monitor project budgets, resource allocation (junior engineers / technicians), and ensure the "man\-hour" targets are met. * Quality Assurance: Implement and maintain ISO quality standards for all deliverables, ensuring "Right First Time" submissions to clients. * Site Inspections: Conduct surveys and oversee site investigation (SI) works to inform the design phase. * Snagging \& Handover: Manage the final commissioning of water infrastructure, ensuring all "As\-Built" drawings and O\&M manuals are accepted by the end\-user. * Management of relationships with key clients and contacts across the business including client liaison and development. * Management of projects within time, cost and quality constraints and standards * Management of the day\-to\-day work of the team. * Champions and promotes professional advice, guidance and support across the business. * Monitors and manages compliance and KPI’s * Estimating, pricing and delivering against work targets. * Management of engineering outsource partners. **What You Bring:** * Exposure to some or all of the following: drinking water treatment and distribution, wastewater collection and treatment, drainage and flood risk management and assessment. * General Civil Engineering Design/Water infrastructure design experience. * Ability to plan, organise and manage resources, provide leadership, mentor and motivate a team of Engineers. * Can effectively communicate with clients, colleagues, external agencies and build effective working relationships. * Commercially astute, prepare technical and commercial bids, win work, project manage and consistently deliver significant projects to program and budget. * Take ownership of multiple projects managing risk, ensuring financial, commercial and contractual compliance. * Able to promote and champion a quality culture to achieve company goals, is committed to Continued Professional Development and keeps up to date with current trends in the industry. * Proven experience in Project Management of large Water Infrastructure Projects. * Experience of contract administration, procurement procedures and contract forms an advantage. * Experience as an External Service Provider (ESP) with Uisce Éireann will be a distinct advantage. * Degree qualified in relevant field * Relevant professional qualification: Engineering/Project Management * Minimum of 10 to 15 years relevant experience * Chartered status or equivalent qualification. * Knowledge of local legislation and its application a benefit. **What We Offer:** We offer a competitive salary package along with a comprehensive range of benefits designed to support your professional growth and enhance your overall well\-being: * **Competitive Salary:** We offer a competitive salary package to attract and retain top talent in the industry. * **Flexible Working:** Enjoy flexible working hours and hybrid working, allowing you to achieve a better work\-life balance. * **Employer Pension Match Scheme:** We offer a pension match scheme to help you secure your financial future. * **Income Protection:** Receive support in safeguarding your income in the event of illness or injury. * **Life Assurance:** Gain peace of mind with life assurance coverage. * **Training and Development Programme:** Access a range of training and development opportunities to enhance your skills and advance your career. * **Family \& Well\-Being Support:** Benefit from our Enhanced Maternity \& Paternity Benefits and our confidential support services to address personal or professional challenges, included in our Employee Assistance Programme. * **Sports and Social:** Engage in our vibrant sports and social activities to foster a sense of community and well\-being among our team members. * **Increased Annual Leave:** 25 days’ annual leave plus statutory bank holidays, enjoy increased annual leave entitlements based on years of service. * **Recognition Awards:** Receive recognition for your contributions through our awards program, celebrating achievements and excellence. *We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.* *We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform crucial job functions, and receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation.*

title: Associate (Transport Planning)

venture partners and framework contracts * Project management and commercial responsibility of proposals * Ensure successful project delivery to time, cost, quality across multidisciplinary teams * Identify, develop and maintain relationships with key clients * Provide support in achieving the targets for work winning and delivery through business development, bid writing and client engagement * Work with clients to understand ultimate objectives and desired outcomes for each project * Ensure the effectiveness of our solution in meeting the client’s needs – with excellent analytical, report writing and presentation skills (working with SYSTRA’s Graphic Design team to prepare high quality materials as required) * Develop and deliver effective stakeholder engagement plans and communications material for individual projects as required, including public consultation * Support/mentor less experienced project/proposal managers * Introduce the latest techniques and be recognised as an expert both internally and externally * Undertake technical training, and lead innovation initiatives * Encourage interaction and cross\-fertilisation of knowledge between technical staff Profile/Skills: * Degree qualification in a relevant subject * Significant depth of Transport Planning experience for winning and delivering complex transport projects, including appraisal, technical report writing, master planning, local transport plans and transport demand management. * Depth of project management experience including client liaison and stakeholder engagement * Familiarity with Transport Modelling tools and techniques would be an advantage * Strong numerical and Excel skills * Experience and passion for mentoring and growing less experienced team members, with line management experience an advantage * Leading multidisciplinary teams * Chartership in relevant profession an example

title: Associate - Civil Engineering
company: CS Consulting Group Civil & Structural Engineers
description: *Dublin \| Senior Leadership Role \| Strategic Impact* CS Consulting is seeking a highly experienced Civil Engineer to join our Dublin\-based leadership team. This is a senior technical and managerial role offering the opportunity to take ownership for the delivery of civil infrastructure on projects while contributing directly to the strategic growth and performance of the business. **Upcoming Projects Include** * Large\-scale residential projects for the Land Development Agency (LDA) and their Development Partners * Private residential projects (high\-rise and low\-rise) nationwide * Refurbishment of historic structures for the OPW and public/ private sector Clients * New\-build commercial office and hotel projects * Commercial office and hotel refurbishment and fit\-out projects * Industrial and data centre projects **The Role** This is a senior, high\-impact engineering role with significant influence across projects and teams. You will lead the design and delivery of civil infrastructure across major projects spanning multiple sectors, combining technical authority with strong commercial awareness. You will manage projects from early concept through to construction and handover, working closely with Directors, clients, and multidisciplinary teams. This role carries significant responsibility for engineering decisions, programme delivery, cost control, and client relationships. You will also play a key role in mentoring and developing engineers within the practice, while representing CS Consulting at a senior professional level. **Candidate Profile** * Masters or Honours degree in Civil Engineering (or equivalent) * Chartered Engineer status with Engineers Ireland and/or ICE * At least 10\+ years’ post\-qualification experience in a consulting engineering environment * Proven experience leading the delivery of all civil infrastructure on a broad range of project types with significant responsibility and strong conceptual, detailed design and construction stage abilities * Experience ensuring designs are developed in accordance with relevant Irish standards, including Uisce Éireann publications, the Greater Dublin Regional Code of Practice for Drainage Works, and Local Authority guidelines * Detailed knowledge of the Irish planning system, typical planning reports, and experience liaising directly with Local Authorities and Statutory Bodies/ Utility Providers such as Uisce Eireann on behalf of Clients at pre\-planning, planning and compliance stages * Experience working with Contractors during the delivery stages of projects including carrying our BCAR inspections to ensure works are completed in accordance with designs, addressing submittals and RFI’s, attending site meetings, closing out snags, etc. * Proficiency using civil design software such as InfoDrainage, Civil 3D, AutoCAD, etc. (or equivalent) * Working knowledge of AutoCAD/ Revit in a coordinated BIM environment. * High level of competence in Microsoft Office and project planning tools * Demonstrated ability to exercise independent professional judgement on complex technical and commercial matters * Strong leadership and people\-management skills within multidisciplinary teams * Sound commercial awareness with experience managing project finances and client relationships * Adaptable, solutions\-focused mindset with a proactive and professional approach * Full Drivers Licence desirable **What We Offer** * Competitive Salary: We offer a competitive base salary and defined bonus scheme, in addition to a generous annual leave allowance \+ company bonus day, and employer pension contribution. * Mid\-Size Firm with Large\-Scale Projects: Opportunity to take ownership of your work with the support of highly experienced Senior Engineers and Directors as well as a talented team of engineering and technician resources. * Broad Range of Projects with the Best Clients: We work across commercial, residential, healthcare, education, hotel, leisure and retail with Ireland's top public and private Clients including the Land Development Agency (LDA), Ronan Group Real Estate, McGarrell Reilly, Quintain, Westridge RE, DRES, Hibernia Real Estate Group, Bartra, Tetrarch, Local Authorities, University of Limerick, Ireland's Top Contractors, and many more. * Shape the way we work: Our Organisation builds on energetic colleagues with a desire to learn, contribute their experience and improve our way of working. We believe in innovation and improvement and utilise data analysis to learn and grow. * CPD Learning: Engineers Ireland approved CPD programme and regular lessons learned and knowledge sharing sessions in a round\-table format. * Leading Software Tech: Access to leading technical software and review our requirements regularly to ensure we are using the best and latest in technology.

title: Associate - Fund Accountant - Private Debt
description: We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed. ****Position Overview**** The Associate (Senior Fund Accountant) of StepStone Private Debt – Fund Accounting in Dublin will be responsible for performing shadow accounting of StepStone funds, will oversee all activities related to a fund on a daily basis and will coordinate and reconcile with the fund administrators. The Senior Fund Accountant will be in contact with professionals across the company and will work closely with team members in other StepStone offices. ****Essential Job Functions:**** * Perform and Maintain monthly valuations of private debt funds and fund of funds (Shadow accounting) * Review management and performance fees calculation * Report and Control of private debt and fund holdings * Budget and track of fund’s fees and expenses * Track and process subscriptions / redemptions, capital calls, withdrawals, dividends, etc. * Collect and reconcile data from and between Investment Advisor, Investment Manager and Fund Administrator; filing and input into the proprietary database * Ensure quality and integrity of data by passing plausibility checks on data inputs * Assist with development of junior staff * Assist in client reports production for a variety of our portfolios and ensuring quality thereof; internal as well as external coordination regarding the content of the individual reports * Perform other duties as requested or as responsibilities dictate * Continuously improve the overall process ****Education And/or Work Experience Requirements:**** * 4\-6 years in a comparable role * Bachelor degree in finance or mathematical\-related subject ****Required Knowledge, Skills, And Abilities**** * Demonstrates commitment, accuracy and attention to detail, even in a high\-volume, fast\-paced environment * Highly motivated with a passion for the industry * Knowledge of the private debt\- / private equity\- or hedge\-fund sectors * Experience in fund accounting and/or custody in one of the above sectors * Passion for fast\-paced learning, especially in a technology\-enabled environment * Good analytical skills with a flair for processes as well as good IT skills * Takes initiative and ownership of responsibilities assigned * Ability to prioritize activities while working on multiple projects and achieve target deadlines * Strong interpersonal and customer service skills ****Other Attributes:**** * Attention to detail * Flexibility—StepStone is a dynamic and entrepreneurial organization; all professionals are expected to perform multiple functions * Promote and demonstrate a positive, team‐oriented attitude * Effective communication skills and strong problem solving skills * Experience with Investran a plus * Highly proficient in using Excel, Word, and other Microsoft office programs * Possess an accreditation like a CFA, CPA, or CAIA * Experience in fund accounting in one of the above sectors * Passion for fast\-paced learning, especially in a technology\-enabled environment * Experience with complex fee calculation models * Strong analytical skills with a flair for processes as well as good IT skills * Takes initiative and ownership of responsibilities assigned * Ability to prioritize activities while working on multiple projects and achieve target deadlines * Strong interpersonal and customer service skills ****Other Attributes:**** * Attention to detail * Flexibility—StepStone is a dynamic and entrepreneurial organization; all professionals are expected to perform multiple functions * Promote and demonstrate a positive, team‐oriented attitude * Effective communication skills and strong problem\-solving skills * Highly proficient in using Excel, Word, and other Microsoft office programs *At StepStone, we believe that our people are our most important asset and crucial to our success. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer that strives to create an environment that empowers our employees and allows them to be heard, regardless of title or tenure. Our organizational community features multiple Employment Resource Groups as well as mentorship programs to enhance the employee experience for all.* *As an Equal Opportunity Employer, StepStone does not discriminate on the basis of race, creed, color, religion, sex, national origin, citizenship status, age, disability, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, genetic information or any other characteristic protected by law.* *Candidates must be at least 18 years old to apply.* Developing People at StepStone

title: Associate Commercial Growth, Account Executive (Arabic Speaking)
description: MongoDB's mission is to empower innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries by unleashing the power of software and data. We enable organizations of all sizes to easily build, scale, and run modern applications by helping them modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our industry\-leading developer data platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi\-cloud database and is available in more than 115 regions across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure. Atlas allows customers to build and run applications anywhere—on premises, or across cloud providers. With offices worldwide and over 175,000 new developers signing up to use MongoDB every month, it's no wonder that leading organizations, like Samsung and Toyota, trust MongoDB to build next\-generation, AI\-powered applications. This role will be based in our Dublin office on our hybrid working model. ### **The Opportunity:** We are searching for an ambitious Associate Commercial Growth Account Executive. Your role will primarily be identifying new workloads to expand MongoDB's usage into accounts that are already leveraging our Database as a Service (DBaaS), Atlas, in a self\-serve manner. You'll focus on helping customers understand the continuous evolution of our developer data platform and its additional features that could be leveraged for their specific use cases. You will support customers at any stage of their journey, fostering greater adoption, retention, and overall satisfaction. This role provides an excellent opportunity for growth in a highly dynamic sales team. ### **Day to Day:** * Identify new workloads and potential use cases within existing accounts to drive increased Atlas consumption via your own pipeline generation and outbound activities * Articulate the value of MongoDB's expanded feature set and evolving developer data platform * Support customers throughout their journey, addressing any queries and ensuring optimal product usage * Develop relationships within customer accounts to promote greater visibility and deepen the partnership between MongoDB and your account's decision\-makers * Strategically navigate diverse industries with a data\-driven discovery and sales process to help land and expand customer workloads ### **What you bring to the table:** * At least 1 years of Sales experience, with a preference for database or software * Consistent record of meeting or exceeding targets in a highly competitive environment (top 10% performer) * Dynamic, enthusiastic, tenacious, team\-oriented mindset * Excellent verbal and written communication skills * Experience working within a quota and commission structure * College degree or equivalent work experience * Native Arabic Speaker * High EQ, coachability, intellectual curiosity, and motivation to build a career at MongoDB ### **Things we love:** * Familiarity with database, developer personas, and open\-source technology * Experience with sales tech stack tools like Salesforce.com, Outreach, and Chorus.ai ### **Why You Should Apply:** * We offer an industry\-leading onboarding process for Associate PLS AE's that prioritizes professional development * High earning potential and a welcoming, inclusive workplace Meet MongoDB's Employee Affinity Groups! * Continuous career development, sales training in MEDDPIC, and Command of the Message * Benefits include: * + Sales Bootcamp + Cross\-departmental mentor and buddy programs * Join us at MongoDB, where your work matters, your individuality is embraced, and you have the freedom to create. You'll be a part of a culture that values your wellbeing and supports your professional and personal journey. We're committed to making a real\-world impact, and we can't wait to see what kind of difference you can make. Learn more about working at MongoDB and help us transform the world! To drive the personal growth and business impact of our employees, we're committed to developing a supportive and enriching culture for everyone. From employee affinity groups, to fertility assistance and a generous parental leave policy, we value our employees' wellbeing and want to support them along every step of their professional and personal journeys. Learn more about what it's like to work at MongoDB, and help us make an impact on the world! MongoDB is committed to providing any necessary accommodations for individuals with disabilities within our application and interview process. To request an accommodation due to a disability, please inform your recruiter. MongoDB is an equal opportunities employer. Req ID: 425464

title: Associate Mechanical DC Engineer
description: **Shape a future with purpose at Arup in Dublin.** **Design the future of sustainable buildings.** Arup’s purpose, shared values and collaborative approach has set us apart for over 75 years, guiding how we shape a better world. Our Building Engineering team sits at the heart of Arup's commitment to sustainable development. We engineer innovative solutions that minimize environmental impact while creating comfortable, healthy, and high\-performing spaces for people to live, work, and thrive in Ireland and beyond. **The Opportunity** Lead a team of passionate engineers and help us revolutionize sustainable building design in a thriving Dublin market. Drive innovation in low\-carbon Data Centre services and contribute to projects that directly address climate change challenges. This role offers significant long\-term career development opportunities within a supportive and collaborative environment. Arup is globally recognised as being at the forefront of the Data Centre and Mission Critical facility industry. **As An Associate Mechanical Engineer At Arup, You Will** * Lead the design and delivery of complex building services projects (science, industry, technology, etc.). * Develop innovative and sustainable mechanical solutions, incorporating net\-zero carbon principles, embodied carbon analysis, and circular economy strategies. * Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams, including architects, clients, and other specialists, to ensure seamless project integration. * Mentor and develop junior team members, fostering a culture of learning and growth within the team. * Oversee contractor coordination, ensuring successful delivery and commissioning of mechanical installations. **Does this role inspire you?** **Qualifications** We're looking for someone passionate about sustainable building design and technically excellent engineering solutions. These are the essential skills and qualifications for the position: * Minimum 12\-15 years of post\-qualification design experience, most of which within a consultancy environment. * Previous design experience in data Centre projects. * Experience in the Irish market (or in the UK). **We Also Value** * Chartered Engineer status (or near chartered) with a proven track record in delivering innovative mechanical engineering solutions. * Strong knowledge of Irish building regulations (including BCAR) and relevant industry standards. * Experience managing contractors and overseeing on\-site work, including commissioning and maintenance considerations. * Excellent communication, teamwork, and leadership skills. **Why Arup?** At Arup, we believe in rewarding our members for their contributions to our shared success. As a trust\-owned company, we have the freedom to pursue meaningful work that aligns with our purpose and values. We also offer a unique profit share program, allowing our members to share in the results of our collective efforts. **Benefits That Work For You** * Professional Growth \& Development: Benefit from continuous learning opportunities, training programs, and mentorship to enhance your skills and advance your career at Arup. * Global Opportunities: Collaborate with colleagues worldwide and explore potential short\-term or long\-term assignments at other Arup offices and project locations. * Financial Well\-being \& Ownership: Receive a competitive salary and benefits package and share in Arup's success through our global profit\-sharing programme. * Wellbeing and Culture: At Arup we are committed to fostering a healthy working culture by offering a range of health and wellbeing benefits, including health insurance, flexible working schemes, wellness programmes and an Employee Assistance Programme. * Equality, Diversity and Inclusion: We strive to embed inclusion in our way of working, our interactions with one another and the projects we deliver. We are committed to creating an inclusive working environment for talented people of any background, based on merit, fairness and respect. At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective where individuality is encouraged. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas. You'll have the opportunity to do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, and to the clients and communities we serve. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to building a workplace that reflects the rich diversity of the world around us. We actively promote a culture of inclusion where everyone feels welcome, respected, and valued. We welcome applications from talented individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, disability, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible. We're here to support you. **Do you want to join us in shaping a better world?** DC2025

title: Associate SIM Engineer
description: **The Company** Cubic³ provides advanced software\-defined vehicle solutions to over 200 countries around the world. Our powerfully smart connectivity enables leading automotive, agriculture, and transportation OEMs to deliver innovative new services and fully compliant in\-vehicle experiences that customers desire, regardless of local market requirements. We believe in leadership that supports empowerment and responsibility, while recognising and developing leadership qualities across Our Team. Together we bring out the best in each other. So, whether you’re interested in joining us as an individual contributor, manager, senior leader – or someone who aspires to growing into a leadership role – we look for people who are results focused, empathetic, visionary, empowering, and who ‘champion’ our cultures and values. **The Role** We are seeking an Associate SIM Engineer who is eager to learn and develop their expertise in SIM and eSIM technologies. You will join a dynamic SIM Engineering team, contributing to the delivery of Cubic solutions in the SIM/eSIM Remote SIM Provisioning technology domain. The Associate SIM Engineer will assist with building connectivity solutions, managing SIM/eSIM card logistics and fulfilment, and supporting our global customer base. You will have the opportunity to gain experience with the latest technologies, including the evolution of eSIM and new connectivity standards. **Responsibilities** * Assist in the design, validation and maintenance of electrical profiles for SIM and eSIM products * Support the design and implementation of Java Card applets for SIM/eSIM services, following 3GPP, ETSI, and GSMA standards * Help write and update technical documentation, such as applet specifications and test plans * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams across mobile core networks, security, and device vendors * Support the development, testing, and integration of SIM\-based applications and tools * Learn to use smart card test \& trace tools * Participate in peer reviews and team meetings **What’s In It For You** * Exposure to end\-to\-end SIM/eSIM ordering and provisioning * Opportunity to work with cutting edge IoT and connectivity technologies * Learning and development opportunities in a collaborative environment * Chance to contribute ideas and develop technical and organisational skills within a small, experienced team **Required Skills \& Experience** * Third level qualification in engineering, computer science, or a related discipline (or final year student/recent graduate) * Up to 2 years of relevant experience, preferably within telecoms technology or similar sectors * Familiarity with SIM/eSIM concepts and protocols (APDU, ISO 7816\) is a plus * Awareness of 3GPP, ETSI, or GSMA standards * Basic understanding of Java, Java Card, or scripting languages * Willingness to learn about mobile network elements and smart card technologies * Quality\-focused, self\-motivated, with analytical and problem\-solving skills * Effective written and verbal communication **Desirable** * Experience using tools like Git, Postman, or smart card test tools (e.g., Comprion) is a bonus * Interest in Kanban/Agile methodologies **Benefits** * 25 days annual leave \+ 2 company days * Health Insurance * Pension Contribution * ESOP * Tax saver scheme * Bike to work scheme * Sports and social activities * Annual office outings * Bits \& Bobs days (quarterly half days for personal tasks) * Learning and Development Opportunities Cubic³ is an equal opportunities employer and committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. *New hires at* Cubic³ *are required to work onsite five days a week during their six\-month probation period to get to know the company, their team, and our ways of working. After probation, we offer a flexible arrangement of up to eight work\-from\-home days per month, provided it aligns with business needs and performance standards. This arrangement is not a given for all roles.* **Cubic³ is an equal opportunities employer and committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace.**

title: Associate, Guidelines Compliance (Dublin)
description: PIMCO is a global leader in active fixed income with deep expertise across public and private markets. We invest our clients’ capital across a range of fixed income and credit opportunities, leveraging our decades of experience navigating complex debt markets. Our flexible capital base and deep relationships with issuers have helped us become one of the world’s largest providers of traditional and nontraditional solutions for companies that need financing and investors who seek strong risk\-adjusted returns. Since 1971, our people have shaped our organization through a high\-performance inclusive culture, in which we celebrate diverse thinking. We invest in our people and strive to imprint our CORE values of Collaboration, Openness, Responsibility and Excellence. We believe each of us is here to help others succeed and this has led to PIMCO being recognized as an innovator, industry thought leader and trusted advisor to our clients. **Position Description** This is an exciting opportunity to work within the Legal \& Compliance department managing investment guidelines in PIMCO’s proprietary compliance system. The Guidelines Team is responsible for interpreting and implementing client, regulatory, and internal guidelines into the compliance system. The successful candidate will have broad exposure to the entire Investment Guideline Compliance process. Individuals in this position will have frequent interaction with a variety of groups at all levels of seniority including other teams within Legal \& Compliance, Account Management, Product Strategy and the Trade Floor. As the role is focused on investment guideline and trading compliance, experience in AML/KYC or financial crime is not applicable to this position. **On a Given Day, Your Work Might Include** * Collaborating with the Client Facing and Legal teams in negotiating investment discretions * Interpreting investment restrictions from different sources (including regulatory frameworks) * Supporting the Trade Floor with investment decisions * Directing the creation of rules within the compliance system * Researching and addressing various inquiries related to investment guidelines * Participating in numerous ad hoc projects tied to investment guideline interpretation **You Will Excel In This Role If You** * Have a strong sense of honesty and integrity * Quick learner and adaptable to learn new processes, concepts, and skills * Exhibit a strong commitment to quality and attention to detail * Possess excellent communication and interpersonal skills * Able to handle multiple, competing priorities and projects **You are going to need this to carry out the job:** * Minimum of a Bachelor’s Degree (Accounting, Business, Finance or Economics preferred) * 2\+ years of professional experience in compliance or the investment management industry * Basic to Intermediate knowledge of the global equity and fixed income markets, including an understanding of financial products such as bonds, equities, and derivatives. * Intermediate/advanced working knowledge of MS Excel. **The following are other qualifications that are preferred but not required:** * Experience with negotiating, interpreting and implementing guidelines in a compliance system is ideal * Knowledge of UCITS or other common legal/regulatory practices is a plus * Experience with Bloomberg, SQL or Business Objects * CFA designation or active status in the CFA program is a plus * German language skills Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action Statement *PIMCO recruits and hires qualified candidates without regard to race, national origin, ancestry, religion (including religious dress and grooming practices), sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, breastfeeding, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender (including gender identity and expression), age, military or veteran status, disability (physical or mental), any factor prohibited by law, and as such affirms in policy and practice to support and promote the concept of equal employment opportunity and affirmative action, in accordance with all applicable federal, state, provincial and municipal laws. The company also prohibits discrimination on other basis such as medical condition, or marital status under applicable laws.* Applicants with Disabilities *PIMCO is an Equal Employment Opportunity/Affirmative Action employer. We provide reasonable accommodation for qualified individuals with disabilities, including veterans, in job application procedures. If you have any difficulty using our online system due to a disability and you would like to request an accommodation, you may contact us at 949\-720\-7744 and leave a message. This is a dedicated line designed exclusively to assist job seekers with disabilities to apply online. Only messages left for this purpose will be considered. A response to your request may take up to two business days.*

title: Banking. Insurance and Customer Service Associate
description: **Contract Type:** Brick and Mortar **Location:** Red Hills Road \- KINGSTON **Date Published:** 04\-30\-2026 **Job ID:** REF21712V Company Description: **About Sutherland:** Artificial Intelligence. Automation. Cloud engineering. Advanced analytics. For business leaders, these are key factors of success. For us, they’re our core expertise. We work with iconic brands worldwide. We bring them a unique value proposition through market\-leading technology and business process excellence. We’ve created over 200 unique inventions under several patents across AI and other critical technologies. Leveraging our advanced products and platforms, we drive digital transformation, optimize critical business operations, reinvent experiences, and pioneer new solutions, all provided through a seamless “as a service” model. For each company, we provide new keys for their businesses, the people they work with, and the customers they serve. We tailor proven and rapid formulas, to fit their unique DNA. title: Bioprocess Technician
description: **About PSC Biotech** **Who are we?** PSC Biotech is a leading Biotech Consultancy firm founded in 1996, headquartered in Pomona, California, USA, with Global operations in Ireland, India, Singapore, Australia and the US, serving 350 clients in more than 23 countries worldwide. We provide cloud\-based software solutions for Quality Management and Regulatory Inspections, pharmaceuticals contract manufacturing professionals, and metrology services to our clients. **‘Take your Career to a new Level’** PSC Biotech disrupts the conventional consultancy model by aligning our EVP as one of the unique selling point which includes the opportunity to work with the most talented cohort of like\-minded professionals operating in the Pharma/ Biotech Industry. We offer a permanent contract of employment giving exposure of working in Top Pharmaceutical client sites in a diverse\-cultural work setting. **Employee Value Proposition** Employees are the “heartbeat” of PSC Biotech, we provide unparalleled empowering career development though Learning \& Development in\-house training mentorship through constant guidance to facilitate career progression. We believe in creating high performing teams that can exceed our client’s expectations with regards to quality of all scalable and business unit deliverables, staying under budget and ensuring timelines for our deliverables are being met. **Overview:** Client will provide Biologics Drug Substance manufacturing for Clinical Supply, Registration \& Commercial Launch. The facility is designed to be operated in a multi\-product mode which requires flexibility to adjust as the pipeline of clinical and commercial biologics\-based medicines demands change. An amazing opportunity has arisen for a Manufacturing Bioprocess Associate to provide operational support for manufacturing operations of the client Late Stage and Launch Pipeline products at our new state of the art single use biotechnology facility . The facility serves as a magnetic force that attracts, recognizes, and integrates people of diverse backgrounds and perspectives. Requirements **Role Functions:** * The Manufacturing Bioprocess Associate will support end\-to end production operations for both Fed Batch and Continuous Manufacturing. **Primary activities/responsibilities:** * Operate equipment according to electronic batch records, sampling plans and standard operating procedures. * Work as part of a multi\-disciplined, diverse and dedicated process team where flexibility and teamwork are an essential requirement * Support weekend or out of hours work on select time frames to support manufacturing activities. * Conduct all work activities with a strict adherence to the safety and compliance culture on site * Support the Authoring of electronic batch records, sampling plans, work instructions and standard operating procedures. **Required Education, Experience and Skills:** * Level 6 qualification in a science or engineering discipline desired. * 2\+ years experience in a GMP Manufacturing requirement shall be deemed equivalent * Ability to work independently and within a cross\-functional team. * Familiarity with contamination control and batch release requirements * Familiarity with Emerson DeltaV, Pas X, and the use of Automation in a manufacturing Process. * Experience with various SingleUse technologies in a manufacturing environment **Preferred Experience and Skills:** * Understanding of Upstream and Downstream Unit Operations for mAb manufacturing **Reports to:** Drug Substance Process Operations Manager

description: Responsibilities of the role: • Engage with business users to define scope, understand business processes, problem statements and pain areas • Identify key stakeholders and construct stakeholder analysis i.e. how they are affected • Support the identification of risks and mitigation approaches • Participate in client requirement workshops and identify gaps together with high level proposals for their resolution (e.g. development workarounds or change in processes). title: Business Insights Analyst
description: **Summary** From our Head Office to the shop floor and everywhere in between our primary goal is to create and deliver a fantastic customer journey for everyone who shops at Lidl. With over 7,000 colleagues across our stores, warehouses and offices in Ireland and Northern Ireland we are a big and ambitious team. We know our people are our greatest asset and our success is down to the commitment and dedication of our team. Across all countries Lidl is currently embarking on a journey to become a data driven organisation. We are seeking a creative, highly motivated and innovative Business Insights Analyst to join our Business Insights \& Analytics Team. We are looking for someone who can leverage key information from large scale data sets and communicate these imaginatively, coherently and effectively. The Analyst will be responsible for reporting the main business performance KPIs to a variety of audiences within the business; the development, generation and distribution of key analysis documentation, reports and dashboards; and the implementation of efficiency initiatives within the Team. They will act as a key contact person for other departments across the business, nationally and internationally. The Business Insights Analyst will report directly to the Head of Business Insights \& Analytics. **What You'll Do** * Undertake daily, weekly and monthly financial and numeric reporting tasks to provide performance updates, analysis and insights into a variety of business areas and initiatives. * Prepare, sense check and issue reporting and analysis documentation as part of a standardised reporting schedule. * Create and maintain reports using Google Workplace products and BI visualisation tools. * Automate existing reporting and analysis practices across the wider business. * Become an expert in our data, analysing all aspects of the company’s performance. * Independent identification and interpretation of abnormalities, variances and trends in performance data. * Analyse relevant business processes to identify and implement continuous and sustainable improvements. * Quality assurance and control of data and systems. * Undertake any necessary administrative tasks within the department. * Contribute to and lead Business Insights projects. * Support the annual financial planning process. **What You'll Need** * Diploma or Degree qualified. * 2\-3 years’ experience in a similar role, with experience of routine financial or numeric reporting. * Superior analytical skills, combined with impeccable business judgment and ability to communicate with senior management. * Advanced Excel skills, e.g. PowerPivot; PowerQuery, task automation through VBA. * Experience with BI tools, e.g. PowerBI, Microstrategy, Tableau. * Ability to integrate into and work as part of a collaborative team. * Proven ability to work in a cross\-functional, fast\-paced environment and to lead and drive complex operational initiatives. * Highly numerate. * Motivated / self\-starter. * Natural inquisitiveness and ability to identify innovation opportunities * Experience with Month End numeric or financial reporting (Advantage). * Foundational knowledge of data analytics ecosystems, with hands\-on experience using SQL, Python, and Apps Script to solve business problems. (Advantage) * Foundational knowledge of Google Workspace products (Sheets, Apps Script, Slides) (Advantage). * SAP (Advantage). **What You'll Receive** We offer a competitive and transparent salary system, that ensures pay equality across all positions at Lidl * €52,500 rising to €67,000 after 3 years * 20 days holidays per annum rising to 25 days after two years * Private employee medical insurance * Company pension * Flexible start and finish times * Initial training and on\-going development from an experienced team member * Excellent opportunities for career progression * Individuals recognised as high performing will be invited to join our Leadership Academy as part of your on\-going development * Dynamic work environment * Modern office facility with free parking * On\-site gym and canteen * Hybrid working model \- up to 2 days remote working per week * Mobile and broadband discounts with Three network * Maternity \& Paternity Leave top up, Marriage Leave, Employee Assistance Programme Lidl is an equal opportunities employer. If you have any particular requirements, we will ensure any reasonable accommodations are implemented as part of the recruitment and selection process. We value diversity and inclusion in our workplace and want to ensure that all applicants have the same opportunity regardless of gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, age, disability, race, religious belief, political opinion or membership of the traveller community.

To meet the unique challenges of global markets, Susquehanna applies machine learning and advanced quantitative research to vast datasets in order to uncover actionable insights and build effective strategies. We build high\-performance systems designed to compete in some of the most demanding and latency\-sensitive markets in the world. guided by speed, capacity, stability, and risk **What We're Looking For** **Skills and Experience** * Proficiency in C\#/.NET and WPF * Experience in at least one additional programming language (e.g. Python or JavaScript) * Solid understanding of software design, debugging, and problem solving * Experience working with distributed systems and business\-critical applications * Familiarity with modern development tools and practices (e.g. Git\-based workflows, CI/CD) * Experience working across the full software development lifecycle, including production support * Strong written and verbal communication skills, particularly in collaborative, fast\-moving environments **Personal Attributes** * Pragmatic and delivery\-focused, without sacrificing code quality or long\-term maintainability * Strong sense of ownership and accountability * Clear, concise communicator with good judgment on when and how to engage others * Curious, adaptable, and motivated to understand both technical systems and business context * Collaborative, professional, and resilient under pressure **Nice to Have** * Experience in financial, trading, or time\-sensitive environments * Exposure to cloud platforms, containerisation, or system monitoring * Experience with databases and writing complex queries * Experience in software performance tuning for high throughput or low latency * Experience contributing to documentation, mentoring, or engineering standards Susquehanna is a global quantitative trading firm powered by scientific rigor, curiosity, and innovation.
