title: Geospatial Analyst
description: **Shape a future with purpose at Arup in Ireland.** Arup’s purpose, shared values and collaborative approach has set us apart for 80 years, guiding how we shape a better world. The Geospatial Data Analytics team provides geospatial analysis and remote sensing data services to both external clients and internal Arup teams working across the built and natural environments. We provide digital leadership focused on the design, delivery and governance of digital technology solutions and services, leveraging our capabilities in geospatial data analytics, information management, enterprise architecture, project management, and emerging technologies. *As part of our multidisciplinary team, you'll contribute to a diverse portfolio of projects across Ireland and beyond.* **The Opportunity** The role involves delivering high\-quality services to a range of prominent clients across Europe. Project work spans diverse areas including the Water, Property, Cities, Transport, Energy and Conservation sectors \- where innovative geospatial solutions are essential. Projects will range in scale from local initiatives to those of global significance. **As a Geospatial Analyst At Arup, You Will** * Deliver mapping and location\-based solutions using ArcGIS Enterprise, ArcGIS Pro, and ArcGIS Online to support major infrastructure projects * Build simple tools and automated workflows in Python (e.g., ArcPy, GeoPandas, Rasterio) to speed up data processing and reduce manual work * Collect, organise, and maintain geospatial data by setting up clear data structures and keeping SQL Server databases accurate and up to date * Analyse spatial data and create models to answer real project questions, helping teams make better decisions for planning, design, and delivery * Turn data into clear outputs (maps, dashboards, visuals) and support colleagues with guidance and training on good data management and cartographic standards **Does this role inspire you?** We're looking for someone who is passionate aboutdigital innovation for sustainable development.We also value: * Bachelor’s degree in GIS or a related field and 2\+ years of hands\-on experience designing and delivering geospatial solutions * Proven experience with ArcGIS Enterprise, ArcGIS Pro, and ArcGIS Online to run spatial analysis and produce standards\-compliant maps * Confident Python development skills for geospatial work (e.g., ArcPy, GeoPandas, Rasterio); bonus if you’ve used ML/deep learning tools (e.g., scikit\-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch) to solve spatial problems * Good data and integration know\-how in infrastructure / built\-environment contexts, comfortable with data management and interoperability (e.g., SQL, FME, ProjectWise, Autodesk Construction Cloud, CAD/BIM); bonus if you’ve worked with ArcGIS Enterprise SDKs/widgets, automation scripts, or 3D visualisation * Consulting\-ready mindset: collaborative, high attention to quality and deadlines, organised problem\-solver, clear communicator, able to manage multiple tasks/projects and eager to build strong client relationships **Why Arup?** At Arup, we believe in rewarding our members for their contributions to our shared success. As a trust\-owned company, we have the freedom to pursue meaningful work that aligns with our purpose and values. We also offer a unique profit share program, allowing our members to share in the results of our collective efforts. **Benefits That Work For You** * Professional Growth \& Development: Benefit from continuous learning opportunities, training programs, and mentorship to enhance your skills and advance your career at Arup. * Global Opportunities: Collaborate with colleagues worldwide and explore potential short\-term or long\-term assignments at other Arup offices and project locations. * Financial Well\-being \& Ownership: Receive a competitive salary and benefits package and share in Arup's success through our global profit\-sharing programme. * Wellbeing and Culture: At Arup we are committed to fostering a healthy working culture by offering a range of health and wellbeing benefits, including health insurance, flexible working schemes, wellness programmes and an Employee Assistance Programme. * Equality, Diversity and Inclusion: We strive to embed inclusion in our way of working, our interactions with one another and the projects we deliver. We are committed to creating an inclusive working environment for talented people of any background, based on merit, fairness and respect. At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective where individuality is encouraged. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas. You'll have the opportunity to do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, and to the clients and communities we serve. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to building a workplace that reflects the rich diversity of the world around us. We actively promote a culture of inclusion where everyone feels welcome, respected, and valued. We welcome applications from talented individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, disability, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible. **Do you want to join us in shaping a better world?**

title: Geotechnical Engineer
description: **Dublin or Cork \| Ireland** **Shape a future with purpose at Arup in Ireland.** ***Engineer the ground beneath tomorrow’s most critical infrastructure.*** Arup’s purpose, shared values and collaborative approach have set us apart for over 75 years, guiding how we shape a better world. Our Ground Engineering team is the largest in Ireland, bringing together geotechnical engineers, hydrogeologists, contaminated land specialists, and engineering geologists. We deliver innovative projects with creativity and passion, shaping the built environment across Ireland and the wider European business. **The Opportunity** This is an excellent opportunity to join a highly specialised team and contribute to a dynamic portfolio of complex, multidisciplinary projects. You will play a key role in shaping some of the most significant infrastructure and development schemes in Ireland and across Europe. While the role focuses on geotechnical engineering design and delivery, you will also gain valuable exposure to the full breadth of Ground Engineering disciplines. This position offers a stimulating and supportive environment to advance your technical, business, and people skills, working alongside industry experts and developing relationships with our firm’s wider engineering community. It also offers the opportunity to place sustainability at the heart of engineering solutions. **As a Geotechnical Engineer At Arup, You Will** * Deliver geotechnical design and analysis for foundations, retaining structures, slope stability, and earthworks on major international commissions (e.g., hyperscale data centres in Europe, the Fehmarnsund Link) and nationally significant Irish infrastructure (e.g., Metrolink, DART\+, N6 Galway Ring Road). * Prepare high\-quality design reports, technical specifications, desk studies, and site walkovers for educational, pharmaceutical, industrial, and commercial developments. * Plan, supervise, and interpret geotechnical ground investigations, providing technical support during the construction phase, including periodic site visits. * Incorporate the latest digital tools, automation, and numerical analysis into our projects to enhance project delivery. * Develop your project management and commercial awareness skills, while supporting and mentoring junior colleagues as you strengthen your own leadership capabilities. **Does this role inspire you?** We're looking for a motivated and ambitious engineer looking to build a strong foundation in geotechnical design. We also value: * An honours Bachelor’s degree in Civil, Geotechnical, or Structural Engineering (or equivalent). A post\-graduate qualification in Geotechnical Engineering or Soil Mechanics is an advantage. * Over 3 years of post\-graduate experience in geotechnical design and/or construction. * Strong technical understanding of the design of deep excavations, deep and shallow foundations, earthworks, slope stability, and soft ground solutions. * Good planning, organisational, and time management skills, with the ability to maintain personal effectiveness across several projects and communicate confidently in both technical and non\-technical settings. * Experience in planning, managing, supervising, and interpreting ground investigation contracts, and/or experience in numerical analysis and GIS. * A commitment to continuous professional development, ideally working towards Chartered status, with the flexibility to travel and work on\-site (a full driver’s licence is a strong advantage). **Why Arup?** At Arup, we believe in rewarding our members for their contributions to our shared success. As a trust\-owned company, we have the freedom to pursue meaningful work that aligns with our purpose and values. We also offer a unique profit share program, allowing our members to share in the results of our collective efforts. **Benefits That Work For You** * Professional Growth and Development: Benefit from continuous learning opportunities, training programs, and mentorship to enhance your skills and advance your career at Arup. * Global Opportunities: Collaborate with colleagues worldwide and explore potential short\-term or long\-term assignments at other Arup offices and project locations. * Financial Well\-being and Ownership: Receive a competitive salary and benefits package and share in Arup's success through our global profit\-sharing programme. * Wellbeing and Culture: At Arup we are committed to fostering a healthy working culture by offering a range of health and wellbeing benefits, including health insurance, flexible working schemes, wellness programmes and an Employee Assistance Programme. * Equality, Diversity and Inclusion: We strive to embed inclusion in our way of working, our interactions with one another and the projects we deliver. We are committed to creating an inclusive working environment for talented people of any background, based on merit, fairness and respect. At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective where individuality is encouraged. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas. You'll have the opportunity to do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, and to the clients and communities we serve. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to building a workplace that reflects the rich diversity of the world around us. We actively promote a culture of inclusion where everyone feels welcome, respected, and valued. We welcome applications from talented individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, disability, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible. If you need any assistance or adjustments, please visit our Candidate Support page for contact information and assistance. We're happy to help. **Do you want to join us in shaping a better world?** Apply here https://www.arup.com/careers/

title: Government Affairs & Public Policy Analyst
description: Government Affairs \& Public Policy Analyst Contract via Coyle Consulting Hybrid (Tuesday\-Thursday in office) ASAP\- August 6, 2026 Our client, a large Tech company in Dublin, is looking for a Government Affairs \& Public Policy Analyst, to join their team for a short term contract, in this role you will complete tasks within the scope of a larger government relations or policy program under some guidance. You perform a range of policy tasks and work with more experienced policy colleagues on research, analysis and execution of policy strategy and initiatives, leveraging your foundational understanding of external environment (e.g., overall industry, socio\-economic and political environment), policy infrastructure, and governing ecosystem, and how they relate to our Policy initiatives and stakeholders. You use your proficient policy process knowledge and influencing skills to identify policy issues that may affect us, monitor, and analyze current and proposed policies, participate in policy campaigns, solve policy and government relations problems, and support policy compliance. **Responsibilities include:** * Monitor and analyze current policy issues and understand their impacts with some support. * Work with business\-area leadership, Legal, Marketing, Trust \& Safety, and PR to support policy\-friendly product development and launch, with some guidance. * Support development and participation in an external campaign with clear strategy and actions to shape a specific policy issue with some guidance. * Support problem\-solving and the development of original policy solutions. * Identify and analyze policy issues that may, today or in the future, affect us, and understand the impact they may have on business and users. **Minimum role qualification requires proficiency in:** * Corporate communications * Policy analysis * Public administration * Policy development * Economics * Community relations * Policy proposal * Stakeholder relations and influence * Regulatory processes * Deal negotiation * Strategic planning and business acumen

title: Graduate Architect
description: A well\-established architecture and interior design practice is now interviewing experienced Architectural Assistants and newly qualified Architects to join its Dublin studio. The practice has built a strong reputation across a varied portfolio, including landmark city\-centre residential towers, sustainable, user\-focused office developments, high\-end hotels, and major infrastructure projects shaping Europe’s future. This breadth of work offers genuine scope for professional development and meaningful creative involvement. Following a strong start to 2026, the studio continues to grow. They are seeking candidates with experience on medium to large\-scale Irish projects and a solid understanding of Irish building regulations. While Revit experience is not essential, it would be a distinct advantage. You’ll be part of a collaborative team working across a range of sectors, including commercial, hospitality, residential, and healthcare. The practice is known for investing in its people and supporting clear, achievable progression into senior roles. The offers a fantastic and competitive benefits package. To apply, or for a confidential discussion about the role, please contact Beth@rhythmcareers.com

title: Graduate BIM Detailer
description: Position: Graduate BIM Engineer Location: Office – Collinstown and Carlow. **Company Description** We are a leading engineering services company with a proven track record of high quality, customer focused project delivery for over 130 years. Our core divisions include Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Sprinkler and Fire Protection. Some of the other services we provide include Waste to Energy (BioEnergy), Specialist Lift, Off Site Fabrication, Technical Support Services, Maintenance, Asset Management, BIM (Building Information Modelling) and Geo\-Surveying. We offer competitive salaries, financial support for further training, Continuous Professional Development (CPD) leading to chartered status, performance incentives and the ability to progress your career with a world class engineering contractor. **Job Description** As a BIM Modeller, you will play a pivotal role in our project development and implementation process. You will work closely with the Planning Manager and Project Manager as well as a multidisciplinary team of engineers and industry experts to deliver cutting\-edge projects. You will be responsible for the creation and maintenance of accurate BIM models of building projects assigned to you in line with the companies and client expectations. Current requirements allow for a base in our offices in Leixlip and Carlow. Your responsibilities will include: * Develop the model virtually for specific design discipline. * Produce detailed 3D BIM models in line with specific project requirements, that offer the best solutions to meet client expectations. * Ensure that the model aligns with the standards and goals set in BIM Execution Plan and BIM project workflow criteria. * Implementing the internal BIM protocols to ensure consistency. * Collaborate and coordinate internally or/and externally with other disciplines design\-changes and model changes. * Create discipline specific BIM Modelling content. * Develop accurate construction drawings and extract data based on the discipline BIM platform. * Coordinate design documentation as a methodology with full utilisation of BIM software and tools. * Attend meetings when needed. * Maintain strong relations with their design team and site teams by frequent site visits, progress updates and resolving issues on the ground. This role will provide exposure to a wide range of expertise relating to pharmaceutical, life sciences, construction, and other sectors. The successful candidate will be supported in developing their knowledge and skills as their responsibilities grow and evolve. **Skills And Requirements** To excel in this role, you should possess the following skills and qualifications: * Level 7 Degree Qualification in a relevant Mechanical Engineering discipline. * Proven experience at Revit (MEP installations) and Navisworks. * Strong team player with excellent interpersonal skills, able to collaborate effectively with colleagues, clients, and external partners. * Excellent organisational and planning skills. * Proficiency in MS Office systems required. * Demonstrated appetite for continuous learning and personal development. **Graduate Development Programme** As part of our commitment to your growth and career development, you will be enrolled in our comprehensive 2\-year Graduate Development Programme. This programme includes experiential learning, mentoring, professional development courses, and in\-house training on key skills such as technical writing, presentation, and time management. You will benefit from peer to peer learning and receive support for obtaining professional certifications. This structured approach ensures you gain the necessary skills and experience to progress in your career. **Benefits** Be part of our Graduate Programme and contribute to meaningful projects that drive innovation and sustainability across multiple industries. We offer a competitive salary package commensurate with experience and qualifications. In addition, you will have the opportunity to work in a dynamic and supportive environment, collaborating with industry leaders and contributing to a sustainable future. Our company values work\-life balance and encourages professional growth through training and development programmes. If you are ready to make a difference and be part of a growing industry, please submit your CV, along with a cover letter highlighting your relevant experience and why you are interested in this role. Recognising the contributions and respecting our people is core to our culture and values. We are an equal opportunities employer, and we encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply for roles. Please note, we are currently not accepting applications from agencies, for this role at this time

title: Graduate Electrical Engineer - Advanced Facilities (Dublin)
description: At Jacobs, we're challenging today to reinvent tomorrow by solving the world's most critical problems for thriving cities, resilient environments, mission\-critical outcomes, operational advancement, scientific discovery and cutting\-edge manufacturing, turning abstract ideas into realities that transform the world for good. Your impact Join the Jacobs Graduate Development Programme (GDP) and have a meaningful, positive impact on our environments, communities and societies. Bring your passion, your ingenuity and your vision. Let’s see the impact we can create, together. What will you get? Our Graduate Development Programme is a structured two\-year scheme designed to support your development at the start of your Jacobs career journey. Through a combination of blended learning, mentoring, progress reviews and networking, you will establish yourself as knowledgeable and competent across four key focus areas; human skills, consulting skills, champion our strategy and your passions. The Graduate Development Programme allows you to customise your learning journey to reflect your specific interests, career goals, and technical skillset – creating a graduate experience that is unique to you. The GDP is an experience programme, meaning that a large portion of what you’ll learn will be on\-the\-job. You’ll work with your manager and mentor to collect the learning experiences that will be most meaningful for you. The programme is more than just a steppingstone. It’s a launchpad for your career, offering you the tools, experiences and support you need. Join us and unlock your potential as you work on meaningful projects and collaborate with industry leaders to deliver outcomes and solutions for complex challenges. **About The Opportunity** This graduate position involves working as part of a multi\-discipline, highly interactive and coordinated team focused on projects including semiconductor manufacturing, data centre facilities and other state of the art manufacturing facilities. Come be a part of a vibrant, dynamic team that delivers world class designs for industry leading clients around the world. We design of some of the most complex and specialized facilities being constructed today. Projects for our clients include feasibility studies, long\-range planning studies, and full design services including programming, schematic design, design development, construction documents, and services during construction for design/bid/build and design/build projects. The ideal graduate will be a self\-motivated individual with strong communication skills. They will exhibit a passion and desire to apply their professional experience and knowledge in a team environment on technically challenging and professionally rewarding projects. Our team is seeking a person who understands the importance of meeting their targeted commitments while being flexible to the ever\-changing and fast paced environment of our clients’ sites. At Jacobs we provide the resources to turn great ideas into reality, and we’re looking for the most talented and passionate individuals out there to be our next generation of leaders. The start date for this position is September 2026\. Here's what you'll need * Be working towards a relevant Degree or Masters level in Electrical Engineering. * A flexible self\-starter who is keen to drive your career forward. * Adaptable to change, with a thirst for learning new things. Apply before 19 October 2025 for your application to be considered in the first assessment window. You can still apply after this date if the role remains open to applications. However, you do so with the knowledge that we may not proceed with your application if the role is filled in the first assessment window. What else we provide * A flexible workstyle, embracing a hybrid working pattern and supporting those who need to balance work/life commitments a bit differently than others. * Reimbursement towards relevant professional development and memberships. * An inclusive and diverse work environment, embracing the variety of disciplines, cultures, backgrounds and lifestyles of our employees through our Jacobs Employee Networks. * A focus on safety, health, and wellbeing, prioritising our people through a culture of caring and positive mental health champion network. * Opportunities to give back to our communities through our Collectively program, incorporating matched\-funding, paid volunteering time and charitable donations. \#EarlyCareersIreland \#GraduateIreland \#GraduateJob2026 \#GraduateCareersIreland \#GraduaterecruitmentIreland \#GraduateJobsIreland \#GraduateopportunitiesIreland Joining Jacobs not only connects you locally but globally. Our values stand on a foundation of safety, integrity, inclusion and belonging. We put people at the heart of our business, and we truly believe that by supporting one another through our culture of caring, we all succeed. We value positive mental health and a sense of belonging for all employees. With safety and flexibility always top of mind, we’ve gone beyond traditional ways of working so you have the support, means and space to maximize your potential. You’ll uncover flexible working arrangements, benefits, and opportunities, from well\-being benefits to our global giving and volunteering program, to exploring new and inventive ways to help our clients make the world a better place. No matter what drives you, you’ll discover how you can cultivate, nurture, and achieve your goals – all at a single global company. Find out more about life at Jacobs. We aim to embed inclusion and belonging in everything we do. We know that if we are inclusive, we’re more connected and creative. We accept people for who they are, and we champion the richness of different perspectives, lived experiences and backgrounds in the workplace, as a source of learning and innovation. We are committed to building vibrant communities within Jacobs, including through our Jacobs Employee Networks, Communities of Practice and our Find Your Community initiatives, allowing every employee to find connection, purpose, and belonging. Find out more about our Jacobs Employee Networks here. Jacobs partners with VERCIDA to help us attract and retain talent from a wide range of backgrounds. For greater online accessibility please visit https//www.vercida.com/uk/employers/jacobs to view and access our roles. As a disability confident employer, we will interview disabled candidates who best meet the criteria. We welcome applications from candidates who are seeking flexible working and from those who may not meet all the listed requirements for a role. We value collaboration and believe that in\-person interactions are crucial for both our culture and client delivery. We empower employees with our hybrid working policy, allowing them to split their work week between Jacobs offices/projects and remote locations enabling them to deliver their best work. Your application experience is important to us, and we’re keen to adapt to make every interaction even better. If you require further support or reasonable adjustments with regards to the recruitment process (for example, you require the application form in a different format), please contact the team via Careers Support.

title: Graduate Landscape Architect (Dublin)
description: **Company Description** **At AECOM, we’re delivering a better world.** We believe infrastructure creates opportunity for everyone. Whether it’s improving your commute, keeping the lights on, providing access to clean water or transforming skylines, our work helps people and communities thrive. Our clients trust us to bring together the best people, ideas, technical expertise and digital solutions to our work in transportation, buildings, water, the environment and new energy. We’re one global team – 47,000 strong – driven by a common purpose to deliver a better world. **Here, you will have freedom to grow in a world of opportunity.** We will give you the flexibility you need to do your best work with hybrid work options. Whether you’re working from an AECOM office, remote location or at a client site, you will be working in a dynamic environment where your integrity, entrepreneurial spirit and pioneering mindset are championed. You will help us foster a culture of equity, diversity and inclusion – a safe and respectful workplace, where we invite everyone to bring their whole selves to work using their unique talents, backgrounds and expertise to create transformational outcomes for our clients. We will encourage you to grow and develop your career with us through our technical and professional development programs and diverse career opportunities. We believe in leadership at all levels. No matter where you sit in the organization you can make a lasting impact on the projects you work on, the teams and committees you join and our business. We offer competitive pay and benefits, well\-being programs to support you and your family, and the development resources you need to advance your career. When you join us, you will connect and collaborate with a global network of experts – planners, designers, engineers, scientists, consultants, program and construction managers – leading the change toward a more sustainable and equitable future. Join us and let’s get started. **Job Description** AECOM is seeking a talented Landscape Architect at the start of their careers to join and support the growth of our Landscape Architecture team. As part of one of the UK\&I’s largest and most diverse landscape practices, you will undertake high\-profile regional and global projects across various sectors including urban regeneration, residential developments, environmental enhancement schemes, and energy projects. Our portfolio includes collaborations with local authorities, private clients, and global developers, delivering innovative and sustainable design solutions that support progress towards net zero and deliver wider environmental and social benefits. This role offers the opportunity to deliver transformative projects, within a talented team, and contribute to AECOM’s reputation for design excellence. With a focus on sustainability, innovation, and collaboration, this position is ideal for a self\-motivated individual seeking to start their career in a dynamic and experienced team. **About AECOM’s Landscape Architecture Team** Our Landscape Architecture team is renowned for excellence and multidisciplinary collaboration. We work across all design stages, blending strengths in design and planning to deliver imaginative solutions that enhance communities and environments. Recent projects include energy transition, public realm, active travel, resorts, and new neighbourhoods that shape cities and regions. **Why Join AECOM?** * **Freedom to Grow**: Take control of your working life with flexible working options, including hybrid working and part\-time hours. Work the hours that suit you. * **Career Development**: Help shape landscape architecture at AECOM with opportunities for training and mentoring. * **Inclusive Culture**: AECOM supports diversity and competitive life\-style benefits. **Job Responsibilities** * **Project Involvement**: + Work collaboratively with colleagues to produce drawings, visualisations and technical documents, including concise reports across all stages of planning, and design projects + Contribute to meetings, workshops and presentations developing creative and practical ideas to help solve complex design challenges. + Assist leads with project management, including financial and programme responsibilities. * **Team Development**: + Develop and share knowledge, supporting a culture of continuous growth and development. + Actively engage in working towards the Irish Landscape Institute (ILI) or UK Landscape Institute (LI) professional exam. * **Client Engagement**: + Assist with nurturing strong relationships with clients, stakeholders, and collaborators. + Facilitate proactive client engagement and business development. * **Technical Excellence**: + Prepare and develop designs and technical materials from initial feasibility through to production of construction information. + Support contract administration to ensure high standard of project delivery. + Produce high\-quality reports and drawings with the necessary level of detail for the various stages of development, using appropriate software. * **Collaboration**: + Work iteratively within multidisciplinary teams to deliver cohesive and integrated design solutions. **Qualifications** **Minimum Requirements** * **Experience**: + Graduates of ILI or LI accredited Landscape Architecture degree. + Demonstrable experience of creating placemaking projects that address key drivers of climate resilience, nature recovery and social inequity. + Knowledge and understanding of the Irish planning system. + Understanding of project management, including financial control, reporting, and programme management. * **Technical Skills**: + Proficient in AutoCAD, Adobe Creative Cloud, and Microsoft Office Suite. + Experience with Rhino, Revit and/or Civil 3D is highly desirable. + Skilled in preparing clear graphics and technical reports. + Strong communication skills, including fluency in written and spoken English. * **Other**: + Full Irish, EU/EEA or UK driving licence. **Preferred Qualifications** * ILI or LI\-accredited Bachelors’ degree, Diploma, and/or Masters’ degree. **Additional Information** * Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible since CVs will be reviewed, and candidates selected on a rolling recruitment basis for either immediate or advanced offer start. * Please include a covering letter with your application and provide all predicted and/or obtained academic grades and details of the dates you are able to work to/from and well as locations you would be interested in. * You must have the right to work in the UK. **About AECOM** AECOM is the world’s trusted infrastructure consulting firm, delivering professional services throughout the project lifecycle – from advisory, planning, design and engineering to program and construction management. On projects spanning transportation, buildings, water, new energy and the environment, our public\- and private\-sector clients trust us to solve their most complex challenges. Our teams are driven by a common purpose to deliver a better world through our unrivaled technical and digital expertise, a culture of equity, diversity and inclusion, and a commitment to environmental, social and governance priorities. AECOM is a Fortune 500 firm and its Professional Services business had revenue of $16\.1 billion in fiscal year 2025\. See how we are delivering sustainable legacies for generations to come at aecom.com and @AECOM. **Freedom to Grow in a World of Opportunity** You will have the flexibility you need to do your best work with hybrid work options. Whether you’re working from an AECOM office, remote location or at a client site, you will be working in a dynamic environment where your integrity, entrepreneurial spirit and pioneering mindset are championed. You will help us foster a safe and respectful workplace, where we invite everyone to bring their whole selves to work using their unique talents, backgrounds and expertise to create transformational outcomes for our clients. AECOM provides a wide array of compensation, benefits and well\-being programs to meet the diverse needs of our employees and their families. We’re the world’s trusted global infrastructure firm, and we’re in this together – your growth and success are ours too. Join us, and you’ll get all the benefits of being a part of a global, publicly traded firm – access to industry\-leading technology and thinking and transformational work with big impact and work flexibility. As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we believe in each person’s potential, and we’ll help you reach yours. We are a Disability Confident Employer and will offer an interview to applicants who have a disability or long\-term condition, who meet the minimum/essential criteria for the role. Please let us know using this email address ReasonableAccommodations@aecom.com if you would like to apply through the Disability Confident Interview Scheme. All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

title: Graduate Program
description: **Launch Your Global Career After Graduation** **Teach English in China \| Full\-Time \| Entry\-Level Friendly** Just graduated (or graduating soon) and not ready for a desk job? Want to travel, gain international work experience, and build transferable skills while earning a competitive salary? **English 1** is hiring **recent graduates and early\-career professionals** to teach English in China. No prior teaching experience required. Full training, visa sponsorship, and structured career development are included. This is an ideal **first job** , **graduate program alternative** , or **gap\-year career launch** for ambitious, globally minded graduates. **What you'll do** * Teach English to young learners aged 3–16 in a fun, interactive, and immersive classroom setting. * Deliver high\-energy lessons using English 1’s award\-winning curriculum and digital tools. * Provide constructive feedback and help students build confidence in their English skills. * Take part in school events, cultural activities, and student engagement programs. **What you’ll get** * Competitive salary \+ performance bonus after 6 months * 21 days paid leave (10 days paid leave \+ 11 days statutory holidays) * Flight allowance * International health insurance (co\-paid) * Airport pick\-up support * \*\*Sponsored legal Z work visa \+ up to 2 weeks sponsored hotel stay * Comprehensive induction training, ongoing support, and fully prepared teaching materials * TEFL certificate \+ skill development \& career advancement * Distance learning courses (Level 6 Trinity College CertPT, DELTM) * English 1\-organized social and cultural activities \+ free Mandarin Chinese lessons *The hotel accommodation for the first two weeks will be deemed as a sponsorship upon successful completion of the first\-year contract. \*\*Sponsored legal Z work visa expenses will be reimbursed.* **Who we’re looking for** * A bachelor’s degree (in any subject) * A valid passport * A genuine interest in education, travel, and cultural exchange. * ESL certification (TEFL, TESOL, CELTA) is a plus\- we can sponsor * Clean background check. **Why work at English 1?** * Teachers are at the core of everything we do at English 1 * Over **30 years** of success in China with **200\+ schools** in **50\+ cities** . * Strong presence in **China** and **Indonesia** . * **Professional development** and career **advancement opportunities** . title: Graduate Quantity Surveyor
description: Join us to help lead the next wave of the digital economy. Our Graduate Programme is a structured two\-year programme that will equip you with the skills and experience required to have a successful career at Apto. As part of this programme, we are recruiting a **Graduate Quantity Surveyor** with a commencement date in early **September 2026** and the role is based in **Dublin, Ireland** . From day one you will gain real hands\-on experience on cutting edge data centre projects while being supported and nurtured by our expert management teams. APTO Data Centres \| What we Do. Apto is a data centre company that designs, builds, and operates hyperscale data centres for our customers in high growth and emerging markets across Europe. Apto has been formed to develop and operate the European data centre assets of global investment manager, PIMCO, benefiting from access to scale, resources and land sourcing capabilities of one of the largest European real estate platforms. Guided by an esteemed leadership team with insights drawn from the world’s digital and data centre titans, Apto brings over a century of combined experience in designing, constructing and operating data centres at scale. From Day 1, the leadership team has focused on the type of company we want to build. We measure success by how well we demonstrate our values every day. We nurture an unfaltering passion for excellence, guided by our customers’ needs and expectations. We obsessively take ownership, deliver on our promises, and create lasting positive impressions. We thrive on overcoming any hurdle to give our customers the best possible experience and outcomes. And, we have fun along the way. Our access to capital and real estate is important but our most valuable asset is our team, and we are delighted now to be recruiting for a Graduate Quantity Surveyor. The role * Support the procurement of Design, General Contractor, Owner Furnished Contractor Installed (OFCI) and third party consultant work packages. * Support the development and validation of construction estimates at various stages of design. * Maintain accurate cost databases and historical cost data. * Monitor cost performance metrics and support monthly cost reporting. * Support the construction team with project close out and final accounts. * Support the management of external consultants. * Collaborate with cross functional teams to ensure effective communication and implementation of commercial processes and procedures. **Basic Qualifications** * Degree in Quantity Surveying or equivalent qualification. Location St Stephen’s Green, Dublin 2, Ireland. Office based at least three days per week. 20% travel across Europe. Employee Benefits * Competitive Salary * Eligible for bonus and Long Term Incentive Plan (LTIP). * 25 days annual leave plus your birthday and bank holidays * Company Pension Contribution * Fully supported and funded professional membership. * Comprehensive Health Insurance for you and your dependents. Life insurance, Critical Illness Cover and Income Protection Reporting Commercial Director **Apto is an equal opportunities employer.** \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ Contact Apto today If you are interested, please apply with your CV! \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

title: Hydrologist and Hydraulic Modeller
description: **Shape a future with purpose at Arup in Dublin.** Arup’s purpose, shared values and collaborative approach has set us apart for over 75 years, guiding how we shape a better world. **The Opportunity** A career at Arup offers you the chance to make a positive difference in the world. Independently owned and independently minded, Arup is the creative force at the heart of many of the world’s most prominent projects in the built environment. We offer a broad range of professional services that combine to make a real difference to our clients and the communities in which we work. For more than 70 years, we have helped bring to life groundbreaking projects across the globe. We are truly global, and our 15,000 planners, designers, engineers and consultants around the world deliver innovative projects with creativity and passion. We have over 800 staff working in Ireland based in Dublin, Cork, Galway and Limerick. Due to significant growth in our Water and Flood Risk Management Business in Ireland and internationally, we are currently recruiting for a **Hydrologist \& Hydraulic Modeller** to join our Irish Flood Risk Management team. The role will support the delivery of a significant number of major flood risk management projects, flood relief schemes and other similar civil engineering projects – all of which have climate change adaptation and resilience as a core aim. The candidate should process a sound understanding of hydrological processes and analysis techniques, as well as a background in hydraulic modelling, both of which are essential success factors for these projects. Arup’s hydrology and hydraulic modelling team provides this insight and plays a key role in the development of sustainable solutions for a wide range of clients, including OPW, local authorities, major infrastructure providers, and private sector clients. Based in our Dublin or Cork office, the ideal candidate will be looking to further their career and gain invaluable experience within an established multi\-disciplinary engineering consultancy in a position of responsibility within a friendly working environment. This is a permanent role and offers the successful candidate long\-term and international opportunities. The successful candidate will play a leading role in delivering a diverse range of projects within the water/flood risk sector. He/she will help build our flood resilience offering in Ireland and as well as internationally. The role will involve leading on a range of technical tasks, mentoring and supervision of more junior staff members, as well as active engagement in skills capacity building in hydrological analysis and hydraulic modelling techniques across the firm. They will also have the opportunity to work with more senior specialists and to link in with our global Water Skills Network to further their own technical development. **Requirements And Skills** * Relevant degree/MSc or equivalent * Strong relevant professional experience * Professional qualification (CEng, CSci, etc.) with one of IEI, CIWEM, ICE or equivalent, or working towards professional status * High level of competency with WINFAP, FEH, FSR, FSU, and similar industry methodologies of hydrological assessment * Experience with hydraulic modelling packages (e.g. TUFLOW, Flood Modeller Pro, MIKE, HECRAS, InfoWorks) and modelling approaches (1D, 2D, combined 1D/2D) * Senior experience on flood risk and water resources projects in the UK or Ireland * Passion for the environment and sustainability * Proven track record in managing, mentoring and training junior engineers * Strong communicator and interpersonal skills * Experienced in project appraisal and authoring technical reports * Experience in preparing Strategic and Site\-Specific Flood Risk Assessments * Preparing hydrology chapters for EIARs * Proven team player with a proactive approach to work **Desirable Skills And Knowledge** * Experience in the use of ArcGIS and /or QGIS * Automation of tasks using digital tools such as python scripts etc. * Water Quality modelling experience for marine environments (i.e. Ecolab modelling in MIKE) **What To Expect At Arup** We offer a multicultural and informal international working environment where you can develop professionally by being part of a team of professionals who enjoy what they do: * Be part of a company with a strong corporate culture committed to its values and aims: quality of work, holistic approach, humane organization, straight and honorable dealings, social usefulness and reasonable prosperity * Short, long or educational assignments at Arup’s offices and projects anywhere in the world * Continuous training and professional development that will allow you to adapt to the growing needs of our clients being in the midst of the digital transformation * Employees at Arup are eligible for the Global Profit Share scheme, dependent on the firm’s financial performance. * We offer a flexible working scheme. We are open to candidates seeking a more flexible working week including flexible start/finish times, planned to work from home and offer the ability to purchase additional leave. Our goal is to shape a better world. And to do that, we need to make Arup a great place to work for everyone. If we want to solve problems for people around the world, we must seek and attract the best people from the widest talent pool and create an environment that supports and includes them. We aim to encourage a culture where people can be themselves and be valued for their contribution. This includes people with disabilities so please discuss any adjustments that you may require throughout the selection and assessment process with your recruitment contact. Shape your time with us At Arup, you’ll be a welcomed member of the team with opportunities to participate in cultural and social events with the local community (e.g. as a STEM ambassador) or by joining one of our staff networks for women, culture or LGBT\+ people. You’ll have access to a range of flexible working options – whether you’re a parent, a student, or just have a busy lifestyle \- we recognise it’s not always easy to commit to standard working hours so if you're interested in flexible start and finish times, working from home or working part\-time, please let us know when you apply, and we'll discuss how this could work in your role. **Why Arup?** At Arup, we believe in rewarding our members for their contributions to our shared success. As a trust\-owned company, we have the freedom to pursue meaningful work that aligns with our purpose and values. We also offer a unique profit share program, allowing our members to share in the results of our collective efforts. **Do you want to join us in shaping a better world?**

title: IMS Engineer
description: **About Telnyx** *Telnyx is an industry leader that's not just imagining the future of global connectivity—we're building it. From architecting and amplifying the reach of a* *private, global, multi\-cloud IP network* *, to bringing* *hyperlocal edge* *technology right to your fingertips through intuitive APIs, we're shaping a new era of seamless interconnection between people, devices, and applications.* *We're driven by a desire to transform and modernize what's antiquated, automate the manual, and solve real\-world problems through innovative connectivity solutions. As a testament to our success, we're proud to stand as a financially stable and profitable company. Our robust profitability allows us not only to invest in pioneering technologies but also to foster an environment of continuous learning and growth for our team.* *Our collective vision is a world where borderless connectivity fuels limitless innovation. By joining us, you can be part of laying the foundations for this interconnected future. We're currently seeking passionate individuals who are excited about the opportunity to contribute to an industry\-shaping company while growing their own skills and careers.* Telnyx is productizing a carrier\-grade IMS core on Kamailio. You’ll design, implement, and operate P\-/I\-/S\-CSCF functions at a global scale— **with VoLTE expertise and the ability to modify Kamailio’s C codebase when needed** . **What You’ll Do** * Design \& build: Own P\-/I\-/S\-CSCF call flows; configure Kamailio modules (ims\_\*, dispatcher, topos, tm, usrloc, permissions); integrate HSS/UDM/UDR. * Productize: Turn prototypes into supportable services with versioned configs, CI/CD, and observability. * Performance \& scale: Tune throughput, dialog/state handling, NAT traversal, DNS/SRV, TLS/mTLS; benchmark CPS/latency. * Reliability: Architect HA/DR; lead load tests, game days, incident reviews. * Security: Implement abuse/DoS controls (pike/htable/rate limiters), auth, encryption, and audit\-grade logging. * Upstream: Contribute fixes/patches and collaborate with the Kamailio community. * On\-call (shared): Keep the voice core healthy. **What You’ll Bring (must\-haves)** * VoLTE expertise: Hands\-on IR.92 (voice/SMS), registration/attach flows, SIP/SDP (AMR\-WB/EVS), QoS/bearers via PCRF/PCF, emergency calling, multi\-vendor interop. * Core IMS/SIP: 5\+ years with SIP/IMS; strong grasp of 3GPP (TS 23\.228, 24\.229, 29\.xx), CSCF roles, registration, service routing. * Kamailio mastery: Confident authoring kamailio.cfg routing logic; registrar/dialog/topology hiding; IMS modules in production. * Software engineering to modify Kamailio (required): * Proficient in C for high\-performance network services (memory management, concurrency, lock\-free/atomic patterns). * Experience building and changing Kamailio core/modules (parsers, transaction/dialog layers, ims\_\*), adding/adjusting AVPs/headers, and writing new module logic. * Tooling: gcc/clang, make, gdb, valgrind/AddressSanitizer, perf/flamegraph, packet tools (pcap, sngrep, ngrep). * Strong unit/functional testing habits; familiarity with Kamailio test approaches and CI. * Comfortable proposing and landing upstream PRs, engaging in code review, and maintaining forks/patch sets. * Systems \& SRE: Linux networking, DNS (NAPTR/SRV), TLS/mTLS, observability (Prometheus/Grafana, ELK/Loki/OpenTelemetry). * Automation: Git workflows, CI/CD, Docker/Kubernetes, IaC (Terraform/Helm), scripting (Python/Bash/Lua). * Communication: Clear design docs and incident write\-ups. **Nice to have** * FreeSWITCH/Asterisk; RTP/RTCP; media relays (rtpengine/rtpproxy); SBC concepts. * Diameter (Cx/Sh/Gx), ENUM/NP; VoWiFi/VoNR; SRVCC/eSRVCC exposure. * Prior upstream contributions to Kamailio or related SIP projects. * Telecom fraud mitigation and signaling security patterns.

title: IT Systems Administrator - North County Dublin
description: **Location:** North County Dublin **Job Type:** Contract 6 Months \- (with option to extend for a further 6 Months) **Work Type:** Hybrid \- onsite presence of 3 days per week for the duration of the contract **Role Type:** Technical / IT Infrastructure \& Security **Overview of Role** The IT Operations Systems Administrator is responsible for the design, build, support, and maintenance of complex Microsoft\-based infrastructure solutions, with a strong emphasis on Cyber Security. Acting as the primary interface between Cyber Security project teams and IT Operations, the role focuses on delivering secure infrastructure initiatives, enhancing organisational security posture, and ensuring alignment with operational and architectural standards. **Role \& Responsibilities:** * Function as technical lead for Cyber Security infrastructure initiatives in a 24x7 operational environment. * Design, build, and implement secure, scalable, and resilient Microsoft\-based infrastructure solutions. * Manage vulnerability remediation and security patching processes. * Produce high\-quality documentation to support audits and compliance requirements. * Perform system administration tasks to maintain system health and stability. * Recommend and implement improvements to operational processes and procedures. * Implement high availability and disaster recovery solutions for critical systems. * Work closely with application teams to ensure integration's are secure, efficient, and perform reliably * Provide escalation support for complex technical issues. * Mentor junior support staff and contribute to team development. * Monitor infrastructure and security performance to meet SLA requirements. * Develop and test fail\-over and resilience strategies for critical services. **Skills, Qualifications \& Experience** * Strong expertise in Microsoft technologies, including Windows Server (2019/2022/2025\) and Active Directory. * In\-depth knowledge of Group Policy, networking concepts, and security best practices. * Experience with backup technologies (NetBackup desirable). * Familiarity with Windows 11 client environments. * Understanding of VMware and storage technologies (desirable). * Exposure to Linux environments (RedHat/OEL desirable). * Strong analytical, troubleshooting, and critical thinking skills. * Experience working within ITIL or structured process environments. * Automation knowledge (Python, Ansible, REST APIs desirable). * Effective communication, documentation, and organisational skills. **Experience:** * 5\+ year's experience in Microsoft infrastructure within a large enterprise environment. * Proven experience delivering secure infrastructure solutions and supporting Cyber Security initiatives. * Experience working in high\-availability, mission\-critical environments. **Education \& Certifications:** * Third\-level qualification in IT or equivalent experience. * Relevant certifications such as MCSA, MCP, CCNA, CompTIA Security\+, or Microsoft Certified Information Security Administrator Associate (Azure/M365 focus). **Visa:** * Irish/EU Citizen or Stamp 4 Visa holder. **About Us** As Ireland's largest IT Solutions Provider with a growing global footprint, ergo offers deep expertise across managed IT \& hardware services, cloud, applications \& software services, IT resourcing, and modern work solutions. Our recent technology awards reflect our commitment to delivering industry\-leading IT solutions, highlighting our team's dedication and expertise.

title: Information Technology System Administrator
description: Senior IT Operations \& Cyber Security Engineer (Contract) Location: Near Dublin (Hybrid – 3 days onsite) Duration: 12\-month contract Day Rate: DOE Overview We’re seeking a Senior IT Operations \& Cyber Security Engineer to support the design, delivery, and optimisation of secure infrastructure within a 24/7 enterprise environment. This role will act as a key bridge between Cyber Security and IT Operations, leading the implementation of security\-driven infrastructure initiatives while ensuring alignment with operational standards and business needs. Key Responsibilities Cyber Security \& Infrastructure Leadership * Act as technical lead on infrastructure\-focused cyber security initiatives * Deliver secure, scalable, and resilient infrastructure solutions * Lead vulnerability management and patching strategies Infrastructure Design \& Engineering * Design and build secure Microsoft\-based environments * Implement high availability and disaster recovery solutions * Ensure infrastructure aligns with security, performance, and SLA requirements Operations \& Continuous Improvement * Perform ongoing system administration to maintain a healthy, stable environment * Monitor infrastructure performance and security posture * Identify and implement process and efficiency improvements Collaboration \& Support * Work closely with internal teams and external stakeholders * Support and resolve escalated technical issues * Mentor junior engineers and contribute to team capability growth Governance \& Documentation * Produce high\-quality documentation for audits and compliance * Support adherence to ITIL and structured operational processes Skills \& Experience Essential * 5\+ years’ experience in enterprise IT infrastructure / systems administration * Strong expertise in: * Windows Server (2019 / 2022\) * Active Directory and Group Policy * Microsoft infrastructure stack * Solid understanding of: * Network protocols * Security hardening * Backup and recovery principles * Experience operating in high\-availability, 24/7 environments * Strong troubleshooting and analytical skills Desirable * Certifications such as MCSA, MCP or CCNA * VMware / virtualisation and storage technologies * Linux (RedHat / Oracle Enterprise Linux) * Automation using Python, Ansible or REST APIs * Experience with enterprise backup solutions

title: Institutional ETF Trading and Execution Analyst | Experienced Hire
description: **Overview** We are a leading quantitative trading firm partnering with institutional clients across global markets. We are seeking an Institutional ETF Trading and Execution Analyst to join our front\-office team. This role combines real\-time problem solving, data analysis, client interaction, and workflow optimisation in a highly quantitative, technology\-driven environment. You will work at the intersection of markets, systems, and people — ensuring trades are executed accurately and efficiently while continuously improving the tools and processes that support them. The role requires exceptional attention to detail, strong analytical capability, and the ability to communicate clearly across technical and commercial teams. **What You’ll Be Doing** * Trade Execution \& Real\-Time Decision Making * Agree and execute ETF trades with institutional clients using structured internal pricing frameworks. * Coordinate with trading teams to ensure accurate and appropriate outcomes * Manage the lifecycle of trades from agreement from pre trade to execution to post trade * Operate in a time\-sensitive environment where precision, discipline, and attention to detail are critical * Accuracy matters — small errors can have meaningful consequences in live markets * Analyse trading and operational data to identify inefficiencies and improvement opportunities * Develop scripts and tools to automate workflows, improve monitoring, and reduce manual processes * Contribute to building scalable processes in collaboration with Devlopment teams * Strong analytical rigour and careful handling of data are essential * Work directly with institutional clients alongside Sales coverage * Explain execution outcomes and respond to technical or operational queries with clarity and structure * Coordinate across trading, operations, sales and technology teams. You will regularly operate at the intersection of multiple teams with different priorities * The ability to communicate precisely, align stakeholders, and avoid ambiguity is fundamental to success **What We're Looking For** * Strong Quantitative problem\-solving ability * Practical programming proficiency \- beginner to intermediate in Python * Comfortable working with structured datasets * Ability to build small tools, scripts, or analytical workflows * High level of precision and attention to detail * Financial markets experience is highly beneficial * An additional language would be advantageous * Prior ETF knowledge beneficial but not required

title: Job Specification for Communications and Engagement Intern  (paid internship)
description: **Overview** Codema, Dublin’s Energy Agency, is currently offering a very exciting six\-month paid internship across its Public and Stakeholder Engagement and Communications and Marketing teams. This internship provides a great opportunity for students or recent graduates in the area of Climate Change, Communications or a similar discipline to gain industry experience and apply what they have learnt in college. The intern will benefit from mentoring by Codema’s experienced staff while developing practical skills and gaining professional experience in one of Ireland’s most respected and leading not\-for\-profit organisations within the energy sector. The successful candidate will get the opportunity to work across several innovative projects and events, such as local community engagement on Decarbonising Zones, EU\-funded community\-focused projects as well as helping to support the organisation and roll\-out of public information events, demos, workshops and conferences. We are committed to leading Dublin's low\-carbon transition and we are looking for someone who is equally committed to these values and realises the key role that effective communication and local engagement plays in achieving this. Codema is a leading energy agency in Ireland, so this is a unique opportunity to gain experience and be at the forefront of driving awareness on the need to move Dublin away from fossil fuels through these various engagement activities and events. **What you will do** You will support the delivery of core communications and engagement outputs across both our Public and Stakeholder Engagement and Communications and Marketing teams. The role will involve tasks and opportunities to learn in areas such as community and stakeholder engagement, event planning, and communications and digital marketing. **Key tasks and responsibilities \- public and stakeholder engagement** ● On\-the\-ground/in\-person engagement with local communities and stakeholders. ○ Responsibilities include stakeholder mapping, preparing an engagement plan and tracking engagements, supporting the running of a local energy advice clinic. ● Support in the organisation of local engagement events and workshops. ○ Responsibilities include supporting Codema’s Public Engagement and Stakeholder Coordinator in designing workshop plans, organising venue and catering, supporting with facilitation, note\-taking and transcribing. ● Support in development and design of communications and engagement materials for local engagement projects ● Support in the integration of impact measurement tools to support engagement activities. ○ Responsibilities include research into suitable impact measurement tools and supporting the trial of relevant tools at engagement activities. ● Support in the creation of a detailed database of potential funding streams to support PASE. **Key tasks and responsibilities \- communications and marketing** ● Event coordination and delivery support ○ Support the Communications and Marketing team to plan, promote and deliver events, including conferences, workshops, EU project events and webinars. Responsibilities include managing registrations, liaising with speakers and sponsors, coordinating logistics and event materials, supporting promotion and stakeholder outreach, and helping ensure events run smoothly online and on\-site. ● Support Codema’s Home Energy Saving Kit and Think Energy campaign activities, including: ○ Providing event support to Codema’s Energy and Climate Awareness Officer, as needed, including at public information stands and Home Energy Saving Kit demonstrations ○ Providing administrative support for the Home Energy Saving Kit scheme, including responding to customer queries, fulfilling orders and managing stock ● Provide digital marketing support across Codema and Think Energy channels, including website, social media platforms and e\-newsletters. This will involve: ○ Creating engaging and creative content ○ Posting regularly across social media accounts ○ Posting news articles and updating website content ○ Assisting in the development of regular e\-newsletter ● Promotional material ○ Support the team with content creation and the design of promotional materials, including brochures, reports, information leaflets and infographics. **We are looking for** ● A team player who has excellent organisational and communication skills ● An eager learner who can hit the ground running ● Someone with a growth\-mindset who will grow and learn from working on real projects ● Someone who is not afraid to ask for help when they need it and will be always supported by the Codema team ● A people\-person with a real ‘can\-do’ attitude ● Outgoing and lots of positive energy **Minimum requirements** ● Currently studying or recently graduated with a Masters or Bachelor’s Degree in Climate Change/Marketing/Communications or similar discipline ● Proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel and PowerPoint ● Good communication skills with the ability to communicate effectively ● Excellent organisational and time management skills ● Fluency in English is essential ● Ability to work with a team and on own initiative ● Eligible to work in Ireland and available to start in June/July 2026 **Additional skills that would be a distinct advantage** ● Interest in the energy and climate landscape in Ireland ● Knowledge and experience of website Content Management Systems (e.g. Word Press, etc.) and social media platforms ● Experience with event organisation ● Knowledge and experience of design platforms such as Canva ● Experience working with a range of stakeholders **Location** The Codema office is located in Dublin city and is accessible by a range of sustainable transport modes. Secure bicycle parking is also available. The internship will be a mixture of remote and in\-office working, with in\-person work required for all events. **Duration** Six\-month full\-time paid internship, commencing June/July 2026\. **Salary** €2,469/month **Next steps** Please e\-mail your CV and a short cover letter to recruitment@codema.ie by 5pm, Friday, 15th May 2026\. As we may be advertising several positions, please insert the title of the position you are applying for in the subject line of your email. We aim to hold interviews the week of 25th May 2026\. **About Codema** We are Codema — Dublin’s Energy Agency. We are driven by our vision to positively impact people’s lives locally, while addressing the environmental damage caused by climate change at a global scale. We believe we can do this by improving the way we produce and use energy locally, while benefiting the people of Dublin. For more information, please visit our website at www.codema.ie. **Codema is an Equal Opportunities Employer**

title: Junior Ecologist
description: The Junior Ecologist supports the delivery of ecological surveys, assessments, and reporting across a wide range of infrastructure, energy, renewables, and development projects. The role provides an opportunity to develop core consultancy skills while working as part of multidisciplinary project teams under the guidance of senior and principal ecologists. **Key Responsibilities** **Field Survey Support** * Assist with ecological field surveys including habitat surveys, protected species surveys, and monitoring work (e.g. GCN, bats, birds, otter, water vole – depending on experience and season). * Support data collection, recording, and interpretation in line with recognised survey guidance. * Follow health, safety, and biosecurity procedures during fieldwork. **Assessment \& Reporting** * Support the preparation of technical reports, including: + Ecological Impact Assessments (EcIA) + EIA ecology chapters + Baseline reports and survey summaries * Assist with data analysis, mapping, and presentation of findings. * Contribute to mitigation measures and recommendations under supervision. **Project Support** * Work within project teams to deliver ecology inputs to agreed scope, programme, and budget. * Assist with project administration, scheduling surveys, and coordination with colleagues. * Support senior staff in responding to client queries and regulator requests. **Learning \& Development** * Develop technical knowledge of UK wildlife legislation, planning policy, and best‑practice guidance. * Participate in training, mentoring, and technical development activities. * Build competency towards independent survey and reporting capability. **Collaboration \& Culture** * Work collaboratively with ecologists, EIA practitioners, planners, and other specialists. * Contribute positively to team culture and knowledge sharing. * Uphold ERM values, quality standards, and commitment to sustainability. **Essential** **Skills \& Experience** * Degree in Ecology, Environmental Science, or a related discipline. * Basic understanding of UK ecology, habitats, and wildlife legislation. * Willingness to undertake fieldwork, including early starts and occasional travel. * Strong attention to detail and good written communication skills. * Ability to work effectively as part of a team. * Full UK driving licence (or working towards). **Desirable** * Some experience of ecological field surveys (e.g. through placements, volunteering, or previous roles). * Familiarity with ecological survey methods and reporting. * Interest in developing specialist skills (e.g. ornithology, freshwater ecology, protected species). * Membership (or working towards membership) of a relevant professional body (e.g. CIEEM). * Basic GIS or data handling skills. **Behaviours \& Attributes** * Curious and keen to learn. * Reliable, organised, and proactive. * Positive attitude to feedback and development. * Commitment to high standards of safety and quality. * Passion for ecology and environmental protection. **Reporting \& Support** * Reports to: Senior Ecologist / Principal Ecologist / Ecology Team Lead * Works closely with: CPD project teams, EIA practitioners, and other technical disciplines **Why Join ERM as a Junior Ecologist** This role offers structured development within a leading global environmental consultancy, exposure to nationally significant projects, and a clear pathway for progression to Consultant and Senior Ecologist roles. Candidates may be invited to complete an online assessment as part of our recruitment process. All personal information will be handled confidentially and in compliance with data protection laws. ⠀ ERM is committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered to thrive, it’s an essential part of what makes ERM a great place to build a career and helps us create better solutions for our clients. We welcome talent from all backgrounds and provide equal opportunities for every candidate. If you have a disability, are neurodivergent, or need accommodations during the selection process, we’re here to support you. Our commitment doesn’t stop at hiring. Once you join us, we’ll ensure you have the tools, support, and adjustments needed to succeed and feel a true sense of belonging. Learn more about our Diversity, Inclusion \& Belonging (DIB) efforts by visiting our website or exploring our 2025 Sustainability Report..

title: Junior Pensions Consultant
description: **Junior Pensions Consultant** **About Your New Employer** Join a leading provider of pensions solutions and be part of an innovative and dynamic organisation focused on employee engagement and investment offerings. As Junior Pensions Consultant you will have the opportunity to work within a team dedicated to supporting thousands across the construction industry with pension, life assurance, and illness benefits. **About Your New Job** * As Junior Pensions Consultant you will deliver engaging presentations (in\-person and virtual) to pension scheme members across multiple schemes. * You will explain pension benefits, options, and retirement processes in a clear, accessible way and help increase member understanding. * You will support member engagement campaigns, respond to follow\-up queries, and promote Additional Voluntary Contributions (AVCs). * As Junior Pensions Consultant you will tailor your communications for different audiences (e.g., site staff, pre\-retirees), maintaining high levels of accuracy and clarity, and collaborate with internal teams to ensure consistency and support the development of communication materials. **What Skills You Need** * QFA qualified * Strong presentation and public speaking skills, with the ability to explain complex topics simply. * Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities, confident with both large and small groups. * Organised and goal\-oriented, able to manage your own diary and work to deadlines. * Relevant experience in pensions, financial services, or employee benefits (preferred). **What's on Offer** The chance to make a real impact by enhancing financial literacy and future readiness for members in a critical sector. Excellent professional development opportunities in a supportive and collaborative environment. Activity target driven role with a focus on engagement and education, not sales or regulated advice. **What's Next** Apply now by submitting your application or contact Aoife Lowe, 01 474 4643, alowe@sigmar.ie. to take your next career step. We welcome candidates seeking a rewarding and meaningful role in a unique, supportive environment.

title: Junior Sous Chef
description: **The Mespil Hotel has an exciting opportunity for a** **Junior Sous Chef** **to join our Kitchen** team. The main responsibilities of this role is to support the Head Chef and Sous Chef in the day\-to\-day operation of the kitchen, ensuring high standards of food quality, hygiene, and presentation are consistently delivered. The Junior Sous Chef plays a key role in supervising kitchen staff and maintaining a smooth and efficient service. This is a challenging position for a highly motivated, hardworking individual interested in developing themselves in the hospitality industry. Areas of responsibility will focus on the following areas: **Kitchen Operations:** * Assist in the preparation, cooking, and presentation of dishes to the highest standard * Support the Sous Chef and Head Chef in managing kitchen operations during service * Supervise and guide Commis Chefs and Chef de Parties * Ensure all food is prepared in line with recipes, portion control, and presentation standards * Maintain a clean, organised, and safe kitchen environment in line with HACCP standards * Monitor stock levels and assist with ordering and inventory control * Minimise food waste and support cost control initiatives * Ensure compliance with all food safety, health \& safety, and hygiene regulations * Assist in training and development of junior kitchen team members * Step in to lead a section or service in the absence of senior chefs * Assist with stock control, ordering, and inventory management * Minimise waste and ensure efficient use of ingredients * Work efficiently in a fast\-paced environment * Maintain a professional appearance and attitude * Adhere to all company policies and procedures **Candidate Requirements** **Key Requirements:** * Previous experience as a Chef de Partie or similar role in a busy kitchen * Strong knowledge of food preparation, cooking techniques, and kitchen operations * HACCP and food safety certification * Ability to work under pressure in a fast\-paced environment and meet deadlines * Strong organisational and time management skills * Team player with good communication and leadership ability * Passion for food, quality, and continuous improvement **Desirable:** * Formal culinary qualification * Experience in hotel or similar kitchen environments * Knowledge of menu development and cost control **Key Competencies:** * Attention to detail * Leadership and teamwork * Flexibility and adaptability * Commitment to quality and standards * Strong work ethic and reliability * Good communication and teamwork skills **Personal Attributes** * Passion for food and hospitality * Positive, can\-do attitude * Reliable, punctual, and professional * Willingness to learn and develop **Working Conditions:** * Shift work including evenings, weekends, and public holidays * Physically demanding role requiring long periods of standing **The benefits of working at the Mespil Hotel:** * Competitive Salary * Continuous training and professional development programs. * Complimentary stays at our sister property. * Additional days off based on your length of service. * Access to our Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) for you and your family. * Option to join our Pension Scheme after 1 year of service. * Option to avail of Health Insurance at reduced rates. * Complimentary buffet\-style meals in the staff canteen for breakfast, lunch, and dinner. * A referral program\-refer a friend to join our team and receive a reward. * Participation in the Bike 2 Work Scheme and the Tax Saver Commuter Ticket Scheme. * Employee recognition initiatives to celebrate your achievements. * An exciting calendar of social events. * Being part of a supportive and dynamic team in a hotel recognized as an "Outstanding Employer" for 2023, 2024, 2025 and 2026 through the Failte Ireland Employer Excellence Program
