title: Junior Structural Engineer
description: **Shape a future with purpose at Arup in Cork.** Arup’s purpose, shared values and collaborative approach has set us apart for over 75 years, guiding how we shape a better world. Our Building Engineering team sits at the heart of Arup's commitment to sustainable development. We engineer innovative solutions that minimize environmental impact and create resilient, sustainable structures. **The Opportunity** Join our Building Engineering team in Cork and contribute to impactful projects across Ireland and Europe. This role provides an excellent opportunity for career development and the chance to work on cutting\-edge structural engineering projects. **As a Structural Engineer At Arup, You Will** * Undertake structural analysis using GSA, Tekla Structural Designer, or other structural software. * Complete the design of structural elements in reinforced concrete, steel, timber, or masonry. * Prepare mark\-ups and work with structural technicians to produce structural drawings using Revit. * Represent Arup at meetings with the client, other consultants and/or contractors. * Conduct site visits and structural investigations. * Prepare reports, presentations, and site reports. * Collaborate with the Design Team on Common Data Environments such as ACC. **Does this role inspire you?** We are seeking a highly motivated and technically proficient individual with a passion for structural engineering and a commitment to sustainable design. **Candidates Must Have** * 1 \- 3 years of post \- graduate experience in structural design. * Degree qualified in Civil/Structural Engineering or similar. MEng or equivalent preferred . **We Also Value** * Excellent communication and report writing skills, including the ability to communicate technical concepts clearly and effectively. * Proficiency in relevant design software packages . * Interest in using and developing digital design tools to deliver smart solutions. **Why Arup?** At Arup, we believe in investing in our people and supporting their growth from day one. As a trust\-owned company, we're driven by our purpose and values, creating a collaborative and rewarding environment for our employees. **Benefits That Work For You** * Professional Growth \& Development: Benefit from continuous learning opportunities, and receive guidance from experienced mentors. * Global Opportunities: Collaborate with colleagues worldwide and explore potential short\-term or long\-term assignments at other Arup offices and project locations. * Financial Well\-being \& Ownership: Competitive benefits package and share in Arup's success through our global profit\-sharing program. * Wellbeing and Culture: At Arup we are committed to fostering a healthy working culture by offering a range of health and wellbeing benefits, including health insurance, flexible working schemes, wellness programmes and an Employee Assistance Programme. * Equality, Diversity and Inclusion: We strive to embed inclusion in our way of working, our interactions with one another and the projects we deliver. We are committed to creating an inclusive working environment for talented people of any background, based on merit, fairness and respect. At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective where individuality is encouraged. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas. You'll have the opportunity to do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, and to the clients and communities we serve. We welcome applications from talented individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, disability, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible. \#IREGrad **Do you want to join us in shaping a better world?**

title: Kubernetes Engineer
description: **Overview** We are seeking a Kubernetes Engineer to join our global infrastructure team and help scale, automate, and secure our container orchestration environments across on\-premises and public cloud platforms. As a Kubernetes specialist, you’ll work closely with DevOps, SRE, and security teams to deliver reliable, self\-service, and production\-ready Kubernetes clusters that power mission\-critical applications. **Key Responsibilities** **Cluster Management** * Deploy, manage, and upgrade Kubernetes clusters using tools like kubeadm, EKS, AKS, GKE, or Rancher. * Implement robust RBAC, network policies, ingress controllers, and security frameworks in Kubernetes. **Automation \& IaC** * Automate cluster provisioning and application deployment pipelines using Terraform, Helm, and ArgoCD. * Build reusable modules for consistent infrastructure delivery across staging and production. **CI/CD Integration** * Integrate Kubernetes with modern CI/CD workflows for rapid, safe application delivery. * Support GitOps practices and continuous deployment automation. **Monitoring, Logging \& Troubleshooting** * Implement observability for Kubernetes using Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, and Fluentd/Fluent Bit. * Troubleshoot performance issues, failed pods, memory leaks, and cluster degradation events. **Cloud \& Hybrid Deployments** * Operate Kubernetes workloads across AWS, Azure, GCP, and hybrid/on\-prem environments. * Use tools like Velero, Kasten, or Stash for backup/restore strategies in Kubernetes. **Collaboration \& Support** * Partner with application developers, SREs, and security teams to implement best practices. * Serve as a technical advisor on cloud\-native architectures and containerization s **Experience/Qualification Required** * Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA) – Highly preferred * Certified Kubernetes Application Developer (CKAD) * Certified Kubernetes Security Specialist (CKS) * AWS Certified DevOps Engineer – Professional * Google Professional Cloud DevOps Engineer * HashiCorp Certified: Terraform Associate * Linux Foundation Certified System Administrator (LFCS) **Nice to have:** * Experience with GitOps tools (ArgoCD, Flux). * Observability tools like Prometheus, Grafana, ELK/EFK stack. * Experience with service mesh technologies (Istio, Linkerd). * Open\-source contributions in Kubernetes\-related projects * Spectro cloud, Incus, Cloud 9 etc… **About InterSystems** InterSystems, a creative data technology provider, delivers a unified foundation for next\-generation applications for healthcare, finance, manufacturing, and supply chain customers in more than 80 countries. Our data platforms solve interoperability, speed, and scalability problems for large organizations around the globe to unlock the power of data and allow people to perceive data in imaginative ways. Established in 1978, InterSystems is committed to excellence through its 24×7 support for customers and partners around the world. Privately held and headquartered in Boston, Massachusetts, InterSystems has 38 offices in 28 countries worldwide. For more information, please visit **InterSystems.com** .

title: Legal Counsel Europe IB
description: **Overview** PepsiCo's Law and Compliance \& Ethics function helps drive business objectives and sustainable results while protecting PepsiCo—legally, ethically, and reputationally. We align with the business, with dedicated lawyers and legal professionals supporting each Sector and BU/market, alongside Global Group/Corporate specialists covering practice areas and governance needs. We work Faster, Stronger, Better by leveraging technology, simplifying processes, and expanding capabilities—while advancing diversity and inclusion within our department and the broader legal profession. By joining the award winning Legal team supporting International Beverages Europe (IB Europe) as Legal Counsel – Europe IB (Franchise), you will be an integral member of the Europe Legal team, providing legal support for our Franchise (FOBO) beverage markets in Europe (excluding Russia), and assisting the General Counsel across all FOBO territories. **Role Purpose** Provide proactive, business centric legal counsel across Europe FOBO markets—acting as a strategic partner to franchise leadership, safeguarding PepsiCo through robust governance, practical risk management, and high quality contracting—while contributing to Europe wide initiatives and standards. **Reporting line:** Reports to the General Counsel, Europe IB **Responsibilities** * Strategic business partnering: Drive timely, high quality legal support for franchise operations; proactively analyze and mitigate risk while enabling growth. * Primary legal point of contact: Serve franchise leaders (Franchise Directors/Managers) and Finance/Control teams as first line independent counsel on day to day matters. * Litigation strategy \& management: Under the General Counsel's supervision, oversee external counsel, set litigation strategy, and implement mitigation plans. * Franchise governance \& transactions: Draft, negotiate, and manage core franchise agreements and amendments, including Bottling Appointments, Concentrate \& Marketing Agreements, and Business Development Agreements. * Commercial contracting: Issue accurate, reliable contracts across franchise, control, supply chain, sales, and finance—aligned to PepsiCo policies, precedents, and controls. * Risk \& corporate governance: Identify and manage legal risk/exposure with third parties; ensure robust corporate governance and complete corporate records. * Policy implementation \& enablement: Implement company policies and provide cross functional support on the documentation needed for effective execution. * Digital \& process excellence: Simplify/automate legal processes; drive the continued roll out of digital tools across Europe IB. * Training \& capability building: Design and deliver targeted training (e.g., anti bribery/anti corruption, antitrust/competition, key contract provisions); uplift legal acumen across client groups. * Collaboration across IB system: Partner with Europe OU Functions, IB Legal, Global Law Department, PPGA, and Trademark teams to apply global precedents and best practices. **Qualifications** **Minimum Qualifications:** * LL.B. (Bachelor of Laws) required; LL.M. is an advantage. * Qualified lawyer, admitted in country of qualification, with minimum 8 years' experience (law firm and/or in house). * FCPA and EU Competition Law: required. * GDPR/data privacy: knowledge is a plus (and familiarity with evolving European privacy frameworks). * Demonstrated experience in corporate compliance, drafting/reviewing/negotiating a broad range of commercial contracts, and stakeholder management across complex, multi market environments. * Fluency in English (spoken \& written); fluency in other EU languages is an advantage. * Ability to prioritize multiple matters to meet business needs; strong computer literacy, negotiation, and communication skills. * Professional, mature, and effective under pressure in fast paced environments; comfortable partnering with senior and junior colleagues. * Team player with a collaborative mindset. **Preferred Skills:** * Strong attention to detail, analytical rigor, and resourcefulness; significant autonomy in decision making with limited supervision. * Self starter who leads projects and delivers on time with clear stakeholder engagement. * Demonstrated leadership—able to work independently and within a collaborative team. * High learning agility—quickly understands local law/regulatory developments and PepsiCo's business; learns from external best practices and cascades knowledge across the team. * Excellent interpersonal, project management, time management, and organizational skills. * Excellent command of computer skills, negotiation and communication skills. * Professional, mature, able to work under pressure in a fast\-paced environment. * Team player who is flexible and confident working with both senior and junior colleagues.

title: Maintenance Contract Sales/Internal Sales
description: **Job Title** **Service \& Maintenance Contracts Sales Executive** (Construction / Automation \& Access Systems) **Department** Sales working with Service / Maintenance Team **Reports To** Sales Director *with strong day\-to\-day liaison with* Service \& Maintenance Manager **Role Summary** This role is responsible for growing and managing revenue from service and maintenance contracts across our installed base of automated gates/doors, barriers, blockers/bollards, folding gates, car parking systems, CCTV and access control. You will: * Convert newly completed installations/upgrades into maintenance contracts by proactively following up recent project customers. * Price and quote additions/extra equipment requested by customers on existing maintenance agreements. * Support the sales team by pricing maintenance contracts for new installation/upgrade quotations. * Generate your own leads and win new maintenance contracts from new and existing customers. The role is primarily office\-based, with regular site visits for customer meetings, surveys and onboarding/handover. **Location \& Travel** * Office\-based at: Dublin Office, Mulhuddart Dublin 15 * Regular site visits across: Mainly Leinster region but we do cover the full RoI * Travel options: use own vehicle (mileage reimbursed) or use company pool cars (subject to availability/policy) * Full driving licence required **Key Responsibilities** **1\) Follow\-up of Newly Completed Installations (Core)** * Track completed installations/upgrades and ensure every eligible customer is contacted within agreed timescales (e.g., within 2–4 weeks of handover). * Introduce and sell appropriate service packages (PPM, reactive cover, SLA tiers, parts/labour options). * Gather/confirm asset and site information needed to contract accurately (asset register, access arrangements, site rules, contacts, billing/PO requirements). * Coordinate internal handover into service delivery once contract is won. **2\) Existing Contract Growth – Pricing Additions \& Variations** * Price and quote additional equipment and contract variations requested by customers (e.g., added gates/doors, extra barriers, additional CCTV/access control doors, intercoms, safety device upgrades, extra call\-out cover, upgraded response times). * Ensure contract documentation is updated with revised scope, asset list, visit frequency and commercial terms as applicable. * Identify upsell opportunities during account conversations (additional sites, additional assets, upgrades to improve reliability/compliance). **3\) Support the Sales Team – Maintenance Pricing for New Works** * Work closely with the new works / projects sales team to price maintenance options alongside new installation/upgrade proposals. * Provide standardised service contract options that align with operational capacity and margin requirements. * Attend joint meetings/site surveys where needed to scope maintenance requirements early. * Ensure maintenance contract proposals clearly state scope, assumptions, exclusions, SLA and pricing. **4\) New Lead Generation – Win New Maintenance Contracts** * Proactively “hunt” for new opportunities through outbound calling, email campaigns, networking, referrals and targeting estates/FM organisations. * Build relationships with facilities managers, property management companies, main contractors, and end users to secure service contracts. * Maintain a healthy pipeline and deliver agreed targets for activity and revenue. **5\) Administration, CRM \& Commercial Governance** * Maintain accurate records in CRM: leads, opportunities, quotes, meetings, next actions and outcomes. * Provide forecasting and reporting (pipeline, conversion rates, order intake). * Ensure all proposals follow internal approval processes (pricing, margin, terms, credit checks where required). * Work closely with service scheduling, engineers, parts/admin and finance to ensure smooth onboarding and billing. **Products / Systems Covered** Automated gates and door automation, folding gates, barriers, blockers/bollards, car parking systems, CCTV, access control and associated safety devices and controls. **Skills \& Experience** **Essential** * Proven experience in B2B sales / account management (construction, service/maintenance, technical products or similar). * Confident in outbound calling and structured follow\-up of warm leads (recent installs). * Strong written communication (clear quotes/proposals) and numeracy (pricing, margin). * Highly organised: able to manage multiple customer follow\-ups, quotes and stakeholders. * Comfortable visiting sites and engaging customers in construction/FM environments. * CRM competence and good Microsoft Office skills. * Determination to succeed and strong interest in closing sales * Full driving licence. **Desirable** * Experience with service agreements/PPM/SLA sales. * Knowledge of automation/security systems (gates/doors/barriers/access control/CCTV) or similar electro\-mechanical systems. * Familiarity with construction/FM processes (inductions, permits, RAMS interfaces, contractor requirements). **Behaviours / competencies** * Proactive and persistent; strong follow\-through. * Customer\-focused, professional, and solution oriented. * Collaborative: works well with sales and service operations. * Detail\-oriented: scopes and exclusions are clear; minimal contract disputes later. **Working Hours \& Package** · Hours: 9am – 5pm Monday to Friday * Salary: Depending on Experience. * Travel: mileage reimbursement or pool car use per policy if available * Laptop * EAP \& Pension (after probation) * 21 days holidays We are looking to grow our Sales Team. If you love selling and providing solutions, this is the role for you. If you have experience of finding prospective customers, building solutions and are always on the look out for opportunities, join our team. Pay: From €45,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company events * Company pension * Employee assistance program * On\-site parking * Sick pay Experience: * cold calling: 2 years (required) * pricing and proposals: 1 year (preferred) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

Our specialist investment capabilities span multiple strategies across private equity, infrastructure and real assets, and private credit. Through our collaborative and committed culture, we find new ways to solve complex problems together and deliver innovative investment opportunities across private markets. Pantheon currently manages approximately $82\.3 billion in AUM across all its strategies, serving more than 750 institutional and 638 private wealth clients worldwide For further details please visit www.pantheon.com **Purpose of Role** The Operational Risk Associate will be part of the wider Enterprise Risk / Operational Risk function and support the team in further developing and managing operational risks across all business functions. The position will report to the Head of Enterprise/Operational Risk. **Key Responsibilities** * Support the development and implementation of Pantheon’s global operational risk frameworks and policies and procedures for adoption by Pantheon Group entities * Support the ISAE 3402/SSAE 16 annual processes including completion of the document, drafting of controls wording and providing external auditor with supporting information. * Support process and control reviews to identify risks and issues in Pantheon’s control environment. title: Packaging Operative
description: **Packaging Operative \- Avoca Central Bakery \& Kitchen Bray** Avoca operates in 13 locations across the country. After all this time, the ethos of Avoca remains the same. We cherish our time\-honoured traditions, which have been lovingly passed down through several generations. Our skills might be steeped in a long and rich tradition, but our attitude is to look to the future. We are now heralded as one of Ireland's most exciting retail stores, with a host of award\-winning cafes, restaurants and food markets crammed with artisanal ingredients from near and far. **Job Summary:** The Packing Operative will be responsible for the day\-to\-day function of the CBK packaging activity. This role is a highly focused on food packaging. A Food Packaging Operative prepares, packs, and labels food products on production, ensuring quality standards and safety regulations (HACCP) are met. Responsibilities include operating machinery, inspecting products for defects, maintaining workstation hygiene, and working efficiently in fast\-paced, between ambient and chilled environments. Requires attention to detail, teamwork, and manual dexterity. **Responsibilities:** * Efficiently pack, seal, and label food products, often at high speeds. * Inspect items for defects, damage, or contamination before packaging. * Set up, operate, and make minor adjustments to packing machinery. * Adhere to strict health, safety, and hygiene standards (PPE required, such as hairnets, bear snood, gloves, coats, safety shoes). * Keep work areas and machinery clean and sanitized. * Complete daily paperwork, including packaging and labelling records. * Move packed goods to racks, dispatch crates or storage areas. **Requirements:** * Attention to Detail. Ability to spot quality issues and follow exact specifications. * Ability to stand for long periods and perform repetitive manual tasks. * Work effectively with colleagues and report faults to supervisors. * Basic skills for reading instructions, labelling, and checking quantities. * Strong work ethic with a focus on punctuality and attendance. * Requires no formal experience (entry\-level), with on\-the\-job training provided. * Previous experience in manufacturing, food, or warehouse roles is advantageous Ability to work in a fast\-paced environment to meet deadlines

title: Partnerships Operations Executive
description: We’re working with a leading residential development platform to appoint a Partnerships Operations Associate within its Partnerships function. This role supports the delivery of large‑scale housing initiatives across Ireland, acting as a key coordination point between internal teams and external partners. You’ll work closely with a Senior Manager to support planning, coordination, and stakeholder engagement across B2G, B2C, and joint venture delivery channels. It’s ideal for someone who enjoys working in a structured, fast‑paced environment and wants to build experience across public and private sector housing partnerships. Key Responsibilities: * Support the coordination of partnership activities, including project timelines and key milestones * Help manage day‑to‑day communication and information flow with internal teams such as development, delivery, finance, and legal * Assist in coordinating meetings, documentation, and follow‑up actions with external partners including government departments, Approved Housing Bodies, and local authorities * Support the management of joint venture relationships and basic tracking of key deliverables * Help maintain trackers, dashboards, and reporting templates to monitor partnership progress and risks * Contribute to improving internal processes and systems within the partnerships function About You: * 5\+ years’ experience in the built environment (for example, real estate, housing, infrastructure, or development) * Familiarity with working alongside or within public sector or B2G environments (e.g. local authorities, housing agencies, AHBs, or related bodies) * Strong organisational and administrative skills, with the ability to juggle multiple priorities * Comfortable using basic project management or CRM tools and working with spreadsheets and reports * Good written and verbal communication skills, with experience drafting clear emails, briefs, and summaries * Someone who is proactive, detail‑oriented, and keen to develop a career in strategic partnerships and housing delivery This is a great opportunity to grow your experience in housing partnerships and play a visible role in delivering impactful housing projects across Ireland. For a confidential discussion click Apply Now or contact cogrady@elevatepartners.ie

title: Personal Lines Account Executive (Junior to Mid-Level)
description: **Responsibilities** * Providing professional best advice and guidance to clients on the most suitable product to meet their Personal Insurance requirements. * Handling new business enquiries for personal insurance and processing quotations. * Evaluate client requirements and provide suitable cover solutions * Deliver first class care and professional best advice to existing clients during and at renewal stage. * Issuing and amending renewals. * Handling Mid\-Term Adjustments, queries and any administration duties that come with servicing clients insurance needs. * Ensuring all compliance standards are met during all processes **Requirements** * Minimum of 1 year experience in personal insurance enquiries for new or existing customers in Ireland. * Hold an Approved Product Adviser (APA) qualification in Personal General Insurance and/or have made progress towards achieving a CIP qualification.

title: Process Technician Apprentice
description: **Process Technician Apprenticeship** ***Are you looking to kick start a career as a Process Technician and get an education while getting paid?!*** If so, Pfizer Grange Castle has an apprenticeship opportunity within a state\-of\-the\-art Biopharmaceutical manufacturing plant for you! *In partnership with the Irish MedTech Association \& IBEC our Engineering network within Pfizer Grange Castle we are seeking candidates for a Process* *Technician Apprenticeship.* *This is a 2\-year program combining part time learning and work placement, leading to attainment of Higher Cert (Level 6\) in Bio Process and Chemical Process Technician.* This programme includes both technical and academic content with a key focus on manufacturing. This is an opportunity to join a world class facility who is dedicated to bringing therapies that will significantly improve patients’ lives. We are looking for an **Apprentice Process Technician** to join our team, this role will support manufacturing operations by assisting with routine production activities, including equipment preparation, material handling, in‑process checks, basic equipment verification. The role includes supporting sampling activities, preparation of materials or solutions for manufacturing use, stock control, and maintenance of production equipment and tools. This role is an on the job, off the job learning. **You will be required to attend college for 15 weeks block release at TU Dublin** **You will return to Pfizer for 9 months. When onsite in Pfizer Grange Castle, you will work on a Shift Pattern which may include Days \& Nights. This will be discussed in advance!** **About the Role and Responsibilities:** You will be directly involved in the manufacturing of our products, ensuring that they meet all specifications for safety, potency, and efficacy. You will work within the guidelines of current Good Manufacturing Practices (cGMP) to ensure conformance with all regulatory requirements and site Standard Operation Procedures (SOP’s), while assisting in the improvement of all current processes through our Continuous Improvement Program. * Assist in maintaining a safety orientated culture, cGMP compliant work environment always. * Perform initial troubleshooting of issues identified during routing operations. * Assist with the investigation of operational deviations through Quality System, engaging with relevant functions as appropriate. * Provide input into the creation and maintenance of area SOPs and batch records. * Preparation of materials for batches and batch manufacture of bio pharmaceutical products. * Use Lean tools as part of daily operations (e.g., Method 1, 5S) to optimise efficiency and drive culture of Continuous Improvement and Zero Defects. * Assist in facility start up, installation and validation activities to support the introduction of new products * This is a shift\-based role (For 24/7 Operations), Day and Night shifts. **Candidate requirements:** * Minimum Leaving Certificate. * Have a passion for Science and/or Technology, Engineering. * Demonstrate a strong work ethic. * Work well as part of a team. * Self\-motivated / self\-starter * Excellence in systems and practical applications. * Have good time management \& prioritisation skills * Be committed to excellence in operational and compliance requirements * Focused on process improvements within a structured environment. * Willingness to learn and grow. * Willing to do Shift Work. * Willing to start September 2026 **Additional Information** * In order to be considered for this position in Ireland you need to be legally eligible to work in Ireland. * Please note there is no relocation support available for this position **How To Apply** * Make a difference today, all suitable candidates should apply with CV below. We are looking forward to hearing from you! Purpose **Breakthroughs that change patients' lives** ... At Pfizer we are a patient centric company, guided by our four values: courage, joy, equity and excellence. Our breakthrough culture lends itself to our dedication to transforming millions of lives. Digital Transformation Strategy One bold way we are achieving our purpose is through our company wide digital transformation strategy. We are leading the way in adopting new data, modelling and automated solutions to further digitize and accelerate drug discovery and development with the aim of enhancing health outcomes and the patient experience. Equal Employment Opportunity We believe that a diverse and inclusive workforce is crucial to building a successful business. As an employer, Pfizer is committed to celebrating this, in all its forms – allowing for us to be as diverse as the patients and communities we serve. Together, we continue to build a culture that encourages, supports and empowers our employees. Disability Inclusion Our mission is unleashing the power of all our people and we are proud to be a disability inclusive employer, ensuring equal employment opportunities for all candidates. We encourage you to put your best self forward with the knowledge and trust that we will make any reasonable adjustments to support your application and future career. Your journey with Pfizer starts here! Support Services

title: Procurement & EPOS Assistant
description: The Wright Group is looking for a Procurement \& EPOS Assistant to support our purchasing and EPOS systems across multiple hospitality venues. This is an entry\-level / early career role suited to someone with some hospitality experience who is looking to move into a head office support function. You will work as part of a team, assisting with day\-to\-day tasks across systems, purchasing, and supplier administration. Full training will be provided. Key Responsibilities **EPOS Systems Management** * Update and maintain EPOS systems across all hospitality venues. * Manage product file changes including PLUs, menus, categories, pricing, and promotions. * Ensure accuracy between EPOS, back\-office, and stock systems. * Coordinate EPOS rollouts across multiple venues. * Troubleshoot issues and liaise with IT or external providers. * Support new system features and integrations. **Procurement \& Purchasing Support** * Assist with supplier ordering, sourcing, and stock processes. * Maintain accurate purchasing records. * Ensure correct pricing and supplier data in all systems. * Monitor stock levels and support decisions. **Supplier Contract Management** * Maintain and oversee supplier contracts. * Verify compliance with pricing, service levels, deliveries, and obligations. * Escalate discrepancies. * Support contract reviews and renewals. **Internal Controls \& Reporting** * Maintain strong control procedures. * Produce purchasing, stock, and EPOS reports. * Assist audits and compliance checks. * Support continuous improvement initiatives. **Skills, Knowledge \& Experience** **Essential:** * Previous hospitality industry experience (any role). * Strong analytical and numerical skills. * Excellent attention to detail. * Strong organisational and communication abilities. * Willingness to travel across multiple venues. **Desirable:** * Full driving licence. **Why work for the group:** * Employee Assistance Program * Fun staff days out * % off our sister sites * Superb training * Competitive salary * Employee Recognition Award * Employee of the month * Bike to work * Strong company culture * Staff referral program * Great opportunities for career progression

title: Product Analyst
description: **Base Location:** You'll be expected to spend 50% of your working week in one of the following locations : Dublin, Reading, Havant, Inverness, Aberdeen, Glasgow and Perth **Salary:** €49,400 \- €74,200 performance\-related bonus and a range of benefits to support your finances, wellbeing and family. **Salary:** £49,004 \- £57,728 and and a range of benefits to support your finances, wellbeing and family. **Working Pattern:** Permanent \| Flexible First options available **Role Overview** As an experienced AI Product Analyst, you will join SSE’s IT Product Analysis team to lead analysis across our AI domain and support the delivery of high profile, customer facing initiatives. You’ll play a key role in driving SSE’s largescale transformation by shaping AI driven products, ensuring strong data protection practices, and guiding teams toward the highest impact opportunities. A core part of this role is staying ahead of the rapidly evolving AI landscape. You are genuinely passionate about AI, continually learning about emerging tools and technologies, and monitoring market and competitor activity to help shape our strategy. **You Will** * Lead analysis across AI and IT initiatives, delivering high\-quality product insights, requirements, documentation, and clear as\-is / to\-be process definitions. * Drive business analysis work across multiple projects, ensuring delivery within expected time, cost, and quality constraints. * Support creation of Power BI dashboards and reporting to provide performance insights and inform decision\-making. * Produce Data Protection and Compliance documentation, identifying and assessing regulatory, data, and technical risks across AI and IT initiatives. * Monitor AI market trends, competitor capabilities, and emerging tools while collaborating with Product Managers, Engineering, Data Science, and Business teams to prioritise and deliver the highest\-value work. **You Have** * Strong understanding of AI/ML fundamentals, responsible AI principles, and the ability to translate model outputs into actionable insights. Skilled in analytics, Power BI (DAX, data modelling) and Databricks. * Solid knowledge of GDPR, privacy by design, data governance, and secure data lifecycle management, ensuring all AI and analytics initiatives meet regulatory and internal compliance standards. * Experienced in writing user stories, defining requirements, documenting as\-is/to\-be processes, applying design thinking, and prioritising high impact work. Skilled in applying Agile frameworks such as Scrum and Kanban, and proficient with Azure DevOps (ADO) and modern product delivery practices. * Broad IT foundation with exposure to data pipelines, cloud platforms, system integrations, and modern application architecture * Genuine passion for AI with a clear plan to stay up to date. Actively tracks new AI tools, market trends, and competitor activity to influence forward\-looking strategy. **About SSE** SSE has a bold ambition – to be a leading energy company in a net zero world. We're investing around £10 million a day in homegrown energy to help power a cleaner, more secure future. Our investment will see us build the world's largest offshore wind farm and transform the grid to deliver greener electricity to millions. Our IT division powers growth across all SSE business areas by making sure we have the systems, software and security needed to take the lead in a low carbon world. They provide expertise, advice and day\-to\-day support in emerging technologies, data and analytics, cyber security and more. **Flexible Benefits To Fit Your Life** Enjoy discounts on private healthcare and gym memberships. Wellbeing benefits like a free online GP and 24/7 counselling service. Interest\-free loans on tech and transport season tickets, or a new bike with our Cycle to Work scheme. As well as generous family entitlements such as maternity and adoption pay, and paternity leave. **Work with an equal opportunity employer** SSE will make any reasonable adjustments you need to ensure that your application and experience with us is positive. Please contact DAvid.brickell@sse.com / 01738 275 846 to discuss how we can support you. We're dedicated to fostering an open and inclusive workplace where people from all backgrounds can thrive. We create equal opportunities for everyone to succeed and especially welcome applications from those who may not be well represented in our workforce or industry. **Ready to apply?** Start your online application using the Apply Now box on this page. We only accept applications made online. We'll be in touch after the closing date to let you know if we'll be taking your application further. If you're offered a role with SSE, you'll need to complete a criminality check and a credit check before you start work.

title: Project Technical Engineer
description: **Job Title:** Project Technical Engineer **Company:** Distag QCS **Location:** Tullow **Reporting To:** Product Line Manager **About Distag QCS:** Distag QCS is a leading provider of components and solutions to Original Equipment Manufacturers \& Distributors. We specialize in Trailer Parts, OEM Bespoke Components, Hydraulic Systems \& Power Transmission components. **Job Description**: As a Project Technical Engineer at Distag QCS, you will play a crucial role in bridging the gap between Supplier, Customer and internal Distag Sales, Purchasing and Technical Teams, developing new products \& working on various projects mainly within our OEM Bespoke Team. You will collaborate closely with our supplier base to ensure they uphold the highest standards of product quality and performance, whilst receiving requests from our Sales team on various projects to develop new products and improve existing. **Key Responsibilities:** * Cross\-functional Collaboration: Collaborate closely with cross\-functional teams, including Sales, Procurement, Engineering \& Quality assurance to ensure alignment on supplier requirements, specifications, and expectations. * Project Management: Develop and maintain project schedules, milestones, and budgets for new product development projects. Coordinate activities across cross\-functional teams, including engineering, manufacturing, marketing, and quality assurance, to ensure timely and successful project execution. * Customer Support: Provide technical support and expertise to internal teams and customers during the product development process and after product launch. Address technical inquiries, troubleshoot issues, and implement solutions to ensure customer satisfaction. * Non\-Conformance Management. Resolve non\-conformance projects by collaboration with internal teams, suppliers and customer. * Continuous Supplier Improvement: Lead and support continuous improvement initiatives aimed at enhancing supplier capabilities, reducing costs, and improving overall product quality and reliability. Collaborate with suppliers to implement best practices and drive process optimization. * Supplier Development: Provide guidance and support to suppliers to help them improve their processes, capabilities, and performance. * Professional Development: Stay abreast of industry trends, emerging technologies, and best practices in new product development. **Qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree in either \-Mechanical Engineering, Industrial Engineering, Business, Supply Chain Management or equivalent experience. * Previous experience in a manufacturing or engineering environment is preferred. * Effective communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build strong relationships with suppliers and internal stakeholders \& customers. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and experience with ERP systems is preferred. * Willingness to travel to customers. Supplier visits in China \& India are not required but desired. **Benefits:** * Competitive salary * Comprehensive benefits package * Opportunities for professional development and growth * Collaborative and dynamic work environment * Chance to make a meaningful impact on product quality and customer satisfaction *Note: This job description is intended to convey information essential to understanding the scope of the position and is not exhaustive. Duties, responsibilities, and activities may change or be supplemented at the discretion of management.* Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Benefits: * Company events * On\-site parking Ability to commute/relocate: * Tullow, County Carlow: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Education: * Bachelor's (required) Experience: * Manufacturing/Engineering: 1 year (required) Work Location: In person

title: QA/Test Analyst
description: **Company Description** Version 1 has celebrated 30 years in business and continues to be trusted by global brands to deliver technology and transformation solutions that drive customer success. Our deep expertise enables our customers to navigate the rapidly evolving technology landscape. We foster strong partnerships with global technology leaders including Microsoft, AWS, Oracle, Red Hat, OutSystems, Snowflake, ensuring that our customers are provided with the highest quality solutions and services. We’re an award\-winning employer reflecting how our employees are at the very heart of what we do: * UK \& Ireland's premier AWS, Microsoft \& Oracle partner * 3300\+ strong, €350/£300m revenue business * 10\+ years as a Great Place to Work in Ireland \& UK * Best Workplace for Women in the UK \& Ireland by GPTW * Best Workplace for Wellbeing in the UK by GPTW We’re a core values driven company, we hire people who share our values, and we reward those who display and foster them, it’s deeply embedded within our DNA. Invest in us and we’ll invest in you!. **Job Description** You may have been working as a Software Engineer in Test, Test Engineer or Test Analyst for a minimum of 4 years and is confident in their ability performing manual test but also enjoy conducting automation testing. You'll be part of an agile team helping our clients achieve their business transformation goals by identifying requirements, developing solutions and providing ongoing guidance and advice in test. **You May Also Get Involved With** * Defining and executing test strategies and scripts * Accurate recording and monitoring of issues * Providing support to the development of scrum teams to automate tests to guide development * Reviewing test environments and processes to facilitate continuous improvement and maintenance of quality standards * Providing test estimates **Qualifications** * Demonstrated experience of Automated Quality Assurance: Deliver overnight cross\-browser, functional, non\-functional, API, integration and acceptance tests. * Delivered front end cross\-browser functional testing, device testing, exploratory, systems integration and user acceptance testing e.g. manual testing. * Confident with building and maintaining frameworks in one or more of these languages: Java, JavaScript, Python, or C\#. * Have practical experience with open source tools such as: Cypress, Karate, PlayWright, Selenium or similar. * Worked in an agile environment. Role is hybrid with a minimum of 2\-3 days in office in Dublin city centre **Additional Information** **Why Version 1?** At Version 1, we believe in providing our employees with a comprehensive benefits package that prioritises their wellbeing, professional growth, and financial stability. * Share in our success with our Quarterly Performance\-Related Profit Share Scheme, where employees collectively benefit from a share of our company's profits * Strong Career Progression \& mentorship coaching through our Strength in Balance \& Leadership schemes with a dedicated quarterly Pathways Career Development programme * Flexible/remote working, Version 1 is tremendously understanding of life events and people’s individual circumstances and offer flexibility to help achieve a healthy work life balance * Financial Wellbeing initiatives including; Pension, Private Healthcare Cover, Life Assurance, Financial advice and an Employee Discount scheme * Employee Wellbeing schemes including Gym Discounts, Bike to Work, Fitness classes, Mindfulness Workshops, Employee Assistance Programme and much more. Generous holiday allowance, enhanced maternity/paternity leave, marriage/civil partnership leave and special leave policies * Educational assistance, incentivised certifications, and accreditations, including AWS, Microsoft, Oracle, and Red Hat * Reward schemes including Version 1’s Annual Excellence Awards \& ‘Call\-Out’ platform. * Environment, Social and Community First initiatives allow you to get involved in local fundraising and development opportunities as part of fostering our diversity, inclusion and belonging schemes. And many more exciting benefits… drop us a note to find out more. Version 1 is an equal opportunities employer. We are committed to building a diverse, inclusive and respectful workplace where everyone feels valued and able to thrive. title: QC Analyst
description: Purpose This role will be part of our Testing Self\-Directed work team in our state of the art single use multi\- product biotech facility in Dunboyne. The QC Analyst will work across the site self\-directed work teams and hub teams to identify and resolve issues in order to allow the site to deliver on our commit culture that support Quality, EHS, Learning and Continuous improvement. The QC Analyst will be responsible for functional testing of in\-process drug substance/drug product, final and drug products, and stability samples at the new single use multi\- product biotech facility. The overall responsibility for this role will be to support the Quality Control testing function related to Biochemistry and Cell Biology methods, ensuring compliance with cGMP and corporate regulations. This is a critical role within the Quality organisation to help establish the Laboratories as a hub for specialised analytical testing and create value for our patients. Responsibilities * Perform a variety of routine Biochemistry and Cell Biology analytical testing techniques and associated documentation; including but not limited to ELISA, PCR, Bioassays and Cell Culture, water and cleaning validation sample testing and compendial methods in compliance with GMP requirements. * Review and approve Analytical Testing data. * Support activities including general lab readiness, audit readiness, laboratory equipment qualification and analytical method technical transfer, verification and validation. * Get actively involved by participating in inspections, GEMBA's and Go\-See's. * Complete COMMIT cards to highlight ways of working within our SDWT's and Hubs across our site that support our COMMIT culture. * Know the hazards of the tasks you are performing, actively conduct risk assessments for inclusion into operational SOPs. * Challenge and identify improvements to the safety and environmental programmes on site. * Be a visible leader of safety initiatives and stay actively involved in safety forums. * Be part of a motivated and high performing team. * Lead approved projects and continuous improvement initiatives that are in alignment with the site strategies * Undertake job specific activities: o On\-time completion for one's own training and to continue professional development and generate expertise in the role. o Ensuring strict adherence to site policies, GMP and environmental, health and safety regulations, compliance with regulatory authority requirements. o Participate in walk\-downs of labs. o Engage in investigations and review of deviations. o Mentor/train new members of staff in specific lab activities. o Assist in project activities as they arise to maintain and optimise the facility. o Testing of all laboratory samples including in\-process and developmental samples, product validation, stability testing. o Performance of laboratory tests as per written procedure or as per pharmacopoeia. o Recording of analytical results accurately. o To act as an end\-user for testing input and process changes. o Operation, qualification, maintenance, calibration, and troubleshooting of laboratory instruments. o Preparation and execution of instrument and method validation protocols. o To maintain written training records. o To ensure that the laboratory is kept clean, tidy and safe always. o To report any non\-conformance, instrument malfunction, accident or other abnormal occurrence to immediate superior. o Ordering, receiving and controlling laboratory chemicals, reagents and consumables. o Receiving and controlling test samples. o Reviewing data, reports, and performing peer reviews to ensure compliance with standards; reports any quality anomalies. o Following all relevant Standard Operating Procedures. o Ensures new/revised quality system requirements are deployed to the site. o Observe GLP/cGMP always. Qualifications * A degree qualification in Science/Quality/Technical (or related) * GMP laboratory experience in the biotechnology and/or pharmaceutical industry, preferably in relation to ELISA, PCR, or Bioassay Analytical Testing. * Preferably Lean Six Sigma experience. * Excellent written and oral communication skills. * An understanding of cGMP requirements for manufacturing and/or systems and compliance. * Required to work on his/her own initiative in a constructive manner in addition to working as part of a team * Proven ability through working across teams focusing on meeting and tracking to schedules and embracing and interdependent work culture as an agile team member.

title: QC Analyst ( 12 Months Agency Contract )
description: Rottapharm Limited Viatris is a global healthcare company uniquely positioned to bridge the traditional divide between generics and brands, combining the best of both to more holistically address healthcare needs globally. With a mission to empower people worldwide to live healthier at every stage of life, we provide access at scale, currently supplying high\-quality medicines to approximately 1 billion patients around the world annually and touching all of life's moments, from birth to the end of life, acute conditions to chronic diseases. We have been included on number of award lists that demonstrate the impact we are making. Our global portfolio includes best\-in\-class, iconic brand\-name products as well as global key brands; generics, including branded and complex generics; and other offerings across a wide range of therapeutic areas. We are committed to helping create healthier communities worldwide through education, outreach and better access to treatment. For those driven to live their lives with purpose, Viatris helps employees realise their full potential by creating a collaborative culture that fosters courage and resilience. Viatris is more than just a place to work. This is a place to make a difference in the world. **The Role \& What You Will Be Doing** Every day, we rise to the challenge to make a difference and here’s how the **QC Analyst** role will make an impact: * Testing of raw materials, intermediate and finished product ensuring that all are testing and data review is carried out within the required timelines and in compliance with guidelines * Drive the right first time and laboratory KPIs to ensure they are achieved * Perform as required documentation updates for QC test methods and procedures * Investigate / Trouble shooting of methods * Assist with improvement projects * Maintain proper care in use and maintenance of equipment * Demonstrate ability of analytical techniques and scientific programs (e.g., LIMS/LabX/UV software/Empower) * Assist with laboratory 6S and maintain good housekeeping * Demonstrate ability in scientific report writing e.g., LIR, Protocols, Reports * Analytical data review * Assist with project management * Perform training when required * No supervisory responsibility **About Your Skills \& Experience** For this role, we’re looking for a candidate who has an effective combination of the following qualifications, skills and experiences: * Associate's degree/college diploma in relevant discipline * Experience of a least one year in a Scientist role. HPLC experience advantageous * Self\-motivated and able to prioritise. At Viatris, we are dedicated to building a truly diverse, inclusive and authentic workplace, so if you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t fully align with every requirement, we still encourage you to apply. You may just be the right candidate for this or other roles. **Diversity \& Inclusion at Viatris** At Viatris, diversity and inclusion are essential to our mission. The diversity we foster in all aspects of our business can be one of our greatest strengths in redefining healthcare not as it is, but as it should be. If you would like to know more about what diversity, equity and inclusion means to us, please visit https://www.viatris.com/en/Careers/Diversity\-and\-Inclusion **Sustainability at Viatris** Corporate social responsibility is fundamental to the Viatris mission. We work to advance responsible and sustainable operations and leverage our collective expertise to empower people to live healthier at every stage of life, recognizing that our actions affect the stakeholders and communities we serve. To learn more about our efforts, please visit https://www.viatris.com/en/about\-us/corporate\-responsibility At Viatris, we offer competitive salaries, benefits and an inclusive environment where you can use your experiences, perspectives and skills to help make an impact on the lives of others. Viatris is an Equal Opportunity Employer.
